title: Field Service Engineer (Omagh, Enniskillen, border regions)
description: At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters. The Position ***Does a rewarding career with a progressive, high\-tech business, excite you?*** Roche Diagnostics has a superb opportunity for you to grow your skills whilst directly supporting our customers in the **Omagh, Enniskillen and border regions** , surrounding areas and being part of the field based Engineering team in Ireland. The successful candidate will provide frontline support covering electro\-mechanical hardware routine proactive/reactive work, technical training, upskilling of customers and ensuring all administration and regulatory documentation is maintained. As the first responder for technical, Applications and IT related issues, you will be expected to build your system understanding around the entire customer solution to include information solutions and applications of the Roche diagnostic products. Key activities will include troubleshooting and ownership of faults, preventative maintenance, fault related data analysis and generation of reports to improve efficiency across Roche’s product portfolio. You will be accountable for the successful delivery/uptime and overall customer experience of their relevant service offering and you will be accountable for the core service activities efficiently and productively, supporting your customer base. You will work with peers and key stakeholders to ensure a seamless “sales\-service” alignment approach and a differentiated customer offering/solution. You will be a key member of the Technical Services Team in Ireland ensuring the right people are in the right place, with the right skills, capabilities and competencies at the right time. You should be resilient, and tenacious. You thrive under pressure, working effectively as part of a close\-knit team in a fast\-paced, time\-sensitive environment with shifting priorities and multiple deadlines. With strong analytical skills, you are at ease working with technical, scientific and numeric information. For this position, it is essential that you are already based in the **Omagh, Enniskillen or border regions** . With this intent in mind, applicants will only be considered if they are currently residing within this geographical area. You will be expected to work closely with the wider team and all other areas of Roche to provide best\-in\-class service for our customers. **Key Responsibilities** * Technical success in the delivery of our service offering for our customers, putting the customers at the centre of what we do and working with other technical managers * Technical effectiveness by championing and upholding technical excellence in the execution of technical activities across systems, processes, data, capability and competence * Technical Customer Centricity by ensuring that we support our customers, react to customer and market demands and continuously strive to achieve technical excellence, inc. continuous improvement and change as necessary * Employee Impact by role modelling flexibility, creativity and teamwork behaviours and building effective cross\-functional/cross\-regional collaboration to multiply your impact **Who You Are** * Ideally Degree\-educated, or Diploma level in Electrical/Mechanical Engineering or equivalent demonstrable experience, you will possess a thorough and up\-to\-date understanding of the IVD industry and have solid experience in implementing best\-in\-class support * With the ability to provide effective support for pathology laboratory solutions, you will be motivated by exceeding customer expectations and instilling knowledge * Self\-motivated with a positive mindset, you will excel in the art of troubleshooting and taking ownership and pride in your work. As an implementer of change, you will have a questioning approach to issues, the ability to drive innovations and train, coach, mentor and develop others **The successful candidate must possess a valid driving licence and have the permanent right to work in Ireland** . Where pay transparency applies, details are provided based on the primary posting location. For this role, the primary location is United Kingdom. If you are interested in additional locations where the role may be available, we will provide the relevant compensation details later in the hiring process. Who we are A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products. We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life\-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact. Let’s build a healthier future, together. The statements herein are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees, and are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of responsibilities, duties, and skills required of personnel so classified. Furthermore, they do not establish a contract for employment and are subject to change at the discretion of Roche Products Ltd. At Roche Products we believe diversity drives innovation and we are committed to building a diverse and flexible working environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion or belief, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, disability or age. We recognise the importance of flexible working and will review all applicants’ requests with care. At Roche difference is valued and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer where you are encouraged to bring your whole self to work.

title: Finance Administrator
description: Finance Administrator \- Our client is an excellent medium\-sized organisation that is working exclusively with the largest Irish companies and government bodies. Due to growth, they require a junior financial administrator to join the team. With a large finance team of 10, this role will be a great job to learn and upskill. Open to IATI or Qualifying as an accountant, they will offer financial support to help. **Main Responsibilities** * Update and maintain the management information database for new instructions * Set up payments for settlements and costs * Maintain updated file reserves * File maintenance on client management systems * Client billing * File review and closures * Bank and credit card reconciliations * Monitor finance email accounts and respond to queries * Assist with month\-end and year\-end close activities and reporting deadlines * Support the finance team with ad\-hoc tasks **Key Skills:** * Previous experience in an office or accounts administration beneficial * Excellent communication and organisational skills * Computer literate with strong competency in Microsoft Outlook, Excel and Word **What we are offering** * Competitive salary and benefits with room for progression * Option to pursue an accounting qualification, if desired * A great team culture * City centre location with excellent public transport links

title: Foundry R&D Software Engineer II
description: **Our Purpose** *Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.* ****Title And Summary**** Foundry R\&D Software Engineer II Who is Mastercard? Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all. **Overview** Mastercard is looking for a talented Software Engineer to join the Mastercard Foundry Research and Development team in our Dublin Tech Hub. In this role you will be part of a highly agile team building exiting and innovative products delivered at scale to global markets. Our team is built on a foundation of research and development, mining innovation internally, innovating new product lines with emerging technology, managing new products from inception to market validation and engaging strategically with start\-ups to shape the future of commerce with and for our customers. At Mastercard you will help define the future of commerce globally. This team will have a diverse focus both in terms of geography and variety of technology challenges driving hard to bring innovative payment solutions to market. Role This Role Involves Working With a Team Of Talented Developers To Develop High Performance Java Based Solutions. In This Role, You Will Be Responsible For: * Design and implementation of innovative capabilities using AI Tooling. * Work as part of an agile team. * Work closely with technical leads and other developers to define solutions. * Research alternative technical solutions to meet changing business needs. * Produce design and research documentation. * Transitioning R\&D solutions to Mastercard core. **Skills** * Proficiency with Java JDK 17 or greater. * Knowledge of Tomcat, Spring, Spring Boot, Shell Scripting, JSON. * Experience with Docker, Kubernetes or other container orchestration solutions. * Expertise in Junit or other automated unit testing frameworks. * Experience with a UI framework such as React. * Knowledge of CI/CD principles and tools such as Jenkins, Gradle, Maven. * Passionate about software development and comfortable in a fluid environment. * Knowledge of Source Control Management and working with code in a multi\-developer environment. * Knowledge of or passion for mobile application development would also be an advantage * Demonstrates hands\-on knowledge of agents, skills, MCP integration, and practical AI workflow concepts. **Other** * Excellent communication (verbal and written) and strong collaboration skills. * Show initiative and demonstrate a willingness to take on challenging opportunities. * Passionate about code quality and coding best practices. * Highly motivated and a strong team\-player. **Corporate Security Responsibility** All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must: * Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; * Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; * Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and * Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Front Desk Associate
description: **About Us** ============ At WeWork, we provide flexible workspace solutions to help businesses of all sizes thrive in more than 37 countries worldwide. The future of work is here, and we’re not just a part of that movement—we’re leading it. United by a desire to build a global community, our purpose is to give members the support to do their best work, and the space to take their ideas to new heights. Join us as we empower tomorrow’s world at work. **About the Opportunity** ========================= As a Front Desk Associate, you’ll be the heart and “face” of WeWork’s hospitality team as the primary point of contact for our members and guests! While no one day will look exactly the same, you will report to one of WeWork’s beautiful locations on weekdays only to support the Community Management team. Depending on the location you are assigned, you’ll either work with a variety of members from different backgrounds and industries, or focus your efforts on one of our Enterprise clients. Employees supporting our Enterprise locations may have different holidays, technical systems, operational and relationship requirements, and property management responsibilities than employees in our Classic locations. **Your work, which will include but not be limited to the duties listed below, will help WeWork to achieve the following:** * Create a welcoming community environment for our members and guests (and sometimes their pets!) by greeting all who enter our spaces warmly. * Make members and guests feel known and cared for by proactively learning their names, answering their questions, and addressing their needs or concerns. * Support the Community Management team with maintaining the front desk operations throughout the day including mail and package handling. * Help ensure your building is fully operational and make adjustments when needed. * Take direction from the Community Associate, Community Lead and the Community Manager to support the Community Team as necessary. * Be an expert in WeWork products and services and appropriately promote WeWork\-provided service offerings to our members and guests. * All of this while illustrating WeWork’s core values and working towards achieving our mission. **In this role, you’ll be responsible for the following areas:** **Front Desk Management** * Greet all members and guests with a warm and welcoming demeanor. * Cover the front desk during the building’s set business hours and be an on\-site point of contact for members, located at the Community Bar. * Ensure visiting guests are registered and checked in and in accordance with WeWork’s guest policies. * Provide orientation of our spaces and relevant information to ensure all first\-time visitors and guests feel welcome and supported. * Support with issuing and collect keys from members and guests as needed. * Keep the front desk clean, organized and free of clutter at all times. * Notify members of any food deliveries and packages. **Member Engagement** * Learn the names of members and guests and get to know their businesses with the goal of building relationships in order to facilitate the sense of Community that WeWork is known for. * Keep track of relevant member notes and information in WeWork systems. * Answer questions from members and guests knowledgeably and be able to offer appropriate WeWork products and solutions to support where relevant. * Identify opportunities to celebrate members’ successes and milestones. * Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures to members and guests. * Know and recommend local restaurants, food delivery services, catering options, team outing venues, post office, shipping center, supply store, etc. **Building Operations and Management** * Assist with the handling mail and packages throughout the day including receiving, processing, sorting and organizing. * Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures around mail and mailroom. * Monitor our spaces and make adjustments when needed, for example, picking up rubbish, resetting chairs, and straightening pillows. * Help maintain building amenities throughout the day, for example, restocking of the Community treat bowl, keycards, printers, etc. * Respond to emergencies and incidents promptly and appropriately, escalating and recording as needed. **About You** ============= We’d love to hear from you if you meet the qualifications below: * You’re a people\-person—you enjoy meeting new people from all walks of life * In\-person interactions energize you and you have outstanding interpersonal skills * You feel comfortable and confident being the face of the brand * Helping others gives you a sense of accomplishment * You work well in a team and can roll up your sleeves to help when needed * You’re able to multitask while always prioritizing the member experience * You’ve worked in hospitality / retail or customer service before which is preferred but not required * You have strong verbal and written communication skills * You are comfortable with and proficient in basic computer skills * You demonstrate and value integrity, dependability, responsibility, accountability, self\-awareness, work ethic, and compassion * You show a passion and understanding of WeWork’s mission and values * You have a secondary school certificate which is preferred but not required **Life at WeWork** ================== Being a WeWorker is more than just a job. We believe the magic of work is sparked by the passion you bring, the places you go, the people you meet and the purpose you follow. And it starts here. Here you will brush shoulders with those who dare to dream and do. Here you will be welcomed by a community that embraces and inspires you—because together we can achieve more. Here we challenge ideas, and explore new ways of getting things done. Whether you are part of our Employee Community Groups , or part of a global project, we ask you to bring your open\-minded attitude and collaborative spirit. In return, you will be part of a team where your unique perspectives are celebrated.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: 🚀 **Java Full Stack Engineer (Contract) \| Dublin \| €480/day (Stamp 4 or Stamp 1G accepted)** We’re hiring a **Software Engineer II (Java Full Stack)** for a leading **global payments \& financial technology organisation** , working on large\-scale, customer\-facing digital platforms. This is a great opportunity to join a high\-performing engineering team building **secure, scalable, and modern digital solutions** used globally. 🔧 **Tech Stack:** • Java (Spring Boot / Spring / Hibernate) • React • Kafka / distributed systems • Cloud\-based application development • Agile / CI/CD environments 📍 **Location:** Dublin (3 days onsite per week) 💰 **Rate:** €480 per day 📅 **Duration:** 12\-month contract (strong potential for extension / perm) ⚡ **Start:** ASAP ✨ **About the role:** You’ll be building and enhancing enterprise\-grade applications, working across both backend and frontend systems in a modern engineering environment, with strong focus on performance, scalability, and reliability. 👤 **Ideal background:** • Strong Java full\-stack engineering experience • Experience with modern web frameworks \- REACT ESSENTIAL • Exposure to distributed systems and Kafka • Comfortable working in Agile, fast\-paced teams 📩 If you're interested, or know someone who could be a fit, please reach out directly for a confidential discussion. \#Java \#FullStackDeveloper \#React \#SpringBoot \#Kafka \#DublinJobs \#TechJobs \#SoftwareEngineering \#ContractJobs \#FinTech

title: Gelato maker - full time fully flexible
description: Gelato maker \- Full Time **30 \+\-** hours a week We at Gino's Gelato are currently recruiting for the role of Gelato Maker to join our team in our store in Blanchardstown Shopping Centre, Gino's is a successful country wide chain of Gelato Shops which is experiencing strong growth and we are currently searching for a customer focused individual to join our dynamic team. If Pride , great quality, and rewarding work appeals to you then apply today to join our team. **Full training provided** Job Types: Permanent Salary: €14\.15\-€14\.75 per hour

title: General Operative (Shift)
description: Become part of our SWARCO team as **General Operative (Shift)** **Challenge** General Operative / Loop General Operative (Shift) About https://www.swarco.com?utm\_source\=chatgpt.com At https://www.swarco.com?utm\_source\=chatgpt.com, we shape the future of transport. As part of the global SWARCO Group, we develop cutting\-edge technologies for intelligent, sustainable, and efficient mobility ecosystems worldwide. From advanced traffic management to intelligent transport systems, our work reduces congestion, enhances safety, and minimises environmental impact. Join our collaborative team, innovating for smarter, greener cities globally. What you will do We are looking to recruit enthusiastic and motivated General Operatives / Loop General Operatives to join our Dublin field operations team. This is a hands\-on, physically demanding role working within a fast\-paced operational environment, supporting the installation, maintenance, and repair of Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) infrastructure across the road network. Working as part of a close\-knit team, you will help deliver safety\-critical roadside and motorway technology that keeps traffic moving safely and efficiently. The role involves a mixture of installation, maintenance, civil works, reactive fault response, and workshop preparation activities. You will work on a shift pattern supporting operational requirements and will play an important role in maintaining high standards of safety, workmanship, and customer service. Shift Pattern * Monday to Thursday: 17:30 – 02:00 * Friday: 17:30 – 01:00 Key responsibilities include: * Installing and maintaining ITS and CITS equipment to a high standard * Carrying out loop cutting, installation, and commissioning works * Attending faults and reactive maintenance activities appropriate to your training level * Assisting with installation and repair works on roadside technology equipment * Supporting civil works associated with operational and project requirements * Preparing, assembling, and testing equipment within the workshop environment * Carrying out lamping, cleaning, inspections, and routine maintenance activities * Completing site surveys and inspections * Ensuring all project paperwork, timesheets, job sheets, and documentation are completed accurately and returned on time * Maintaining company vehicles, tools, equipment, and PPE in a safe and serviceable condition * Supporting trainees, apprentices, and colleagues through guidance and knowledge sharing * Following all company Quality, Health \& Safety, Security and Environmental procedures at all times On a day\-to\-day basis, you will: * Work outdoors on live roadside and infrastructure environments * Support installation and maintenance activities across ITS projects and reactive works * Carry out physical and practical tasks including civil works and equipment installation * Work safely at heights and within live traffic environments * Ensure safe working practices and RAMS are followed at all times * Prepare and organise materials, tools, equipment, and vehicles for daily activities * Attend training courses and toolbox talks to support ongoing development * Work collaboratively with technicians, engineers, and operational teams to deliver successful project outcomes * Assist in achieving maintenance KPIs and operational targets **Profile** What we are looking for Essential: * Civils, construction, or infrastructure experience * Full B \& C driving licence * Machine or plant operator experience * Ability to work outdoors in a physical role * Ability to work at heights * Ability to work effectively as part of a team * Flexible approach to shift work, standby, and callout rotas * Strong safety awareness and commitment to safe working practices * SOLAS Safe Pass * Reliable, motivated, and professional attitude Desirable: * Electrical or electronic servicing training * Basic understanding of ITS site installations * Abrasive Wheels training * CSCS Signing, Lighting \& Guarding at Roadworks * CSCS Location of Underground Services * Previous experience within the traffic industry or similar field\-based environment * Good mechanical aptitude * Willingness to learn and take on new challenges **Benefit** What we offer As well as providing a competitive salary and benefits package, SWARCO actively runs employee opinion surveys as part of our drive to provide a comfortable and supportive working environment. We are committed to supporting the ongoing development of our employees and creating long\-term career opportunities within the business. You will also receive: * Competitive salary of €42,000 \- €45,000 * 20% shift allowance * Structured training and development opportunities * Opportunity to work within a specialist ITS and transport technology environment * Supportive team culture and collaborative working environment * Company PPE, tools, and equipment * Career progression opportunities within SWARCO Interested? Please apply by clicking on the apply button. Applications are shortlisted on a rolling basis, and we reserve the right to interview and appoint before any advertised closing date. We therefore encourage you to apply at the earliest opportunity to avoid disappointment. Applications received after the closing date will not be processed. We have a responsibility to ensure that all employees are eligible to live and work in Ireland, therefore successful candidates must have the right to work in Ireland by the start of their employment. We are an equal opportunities employer. Become a part of SWARCO. We look forward to receiving your comprehensive application via the online tool. **SWARCO Ireland Ltd \- SWARCO** Essmore House 4 Kylemore Business Park Kylemore Way Dublin 8 DO8 EWP2 Ireland *Information on processing of your personal data is available https://www.swarco.com/privacy\-information\-applicants.*

title: Graduate BIM Detailer
description: Position: Graduate BIM Engineer Location: Office – Collinstown and Carlow. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a BIM Modeller, you will play a pivotal role in our project development and implementation process. You will work closely with the Planning Manager and Project Manager as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. You will be responsible for the creation and maintenance of accurate BIM models of building projects assigned to you in line with the companies and client expectations. Current requirements allow for a base in our offices in Leixlip and Carlow. Your responsibilities will include: * Develop the model virtually for specific design discipline. * Produce detailed 3D BIM models in line with specific project requirements, that offer the best solutions to meet client expectations. * Ensure that the model aligns with the standards and goals set in BIM Execution Plan and BIM project workflow criteria. * Implementing the internal BIM protocols to ensure consistency. * Collaborate and coordinate internally or/and externally with other disciplines design\-changes and model changes. * Create discipline specific BIM Modelling content. * Develop accurate construction drawings and extract data based on the discipline BIM platform. * Coordinate design documentation as a methodology with full utilisation of BIM software and tools. * Attend meetings when needed. * Maintain strong relations with their design team and site teams by frequent site visits, progress updates and resolving issues on the ground. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to pharmaceutical, life sciences, construction, and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Level 7 Degree Qualification in a relevant Mechanical Engineering discipline. * Proven experience at Revit (MEP installations) and Navisworks. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems required. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programmes. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: LotusWorks is an Engineering Services provider specializing in managing Commissioning, Construction Services, Calibration, Operations \& Maintenance scopes of work within global manufacturing facilities. LotusWorks operates globally across EMEA and North America partnering with the world's leading manufacturers across the Semiconductor, Pharmaceutical \& Biologics, Medical Device and Data Centre sectors. Our team of Engineering and Technical professionals is immersed in cutting\-edge technologies and innovations. LotusWorks is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, integral to our people\-first approach. As a Graduate Electrical Engineer (Commissioning), you will support the Electrical Commissioning Team in the execution of electrical commissioning activities on Data Centre projects across Europe. This role combines hands\-on field commissioning with office\-based engineering support, providing an excellent opportunity to develop technical expertise in electrical systems, infrastructure, controls, and commissioning processes. Working alongside experienced engineers and technicians, you will receive structured training, mentoring, and field exposure to build competence in commissioning activities from Level 2 (Installation Verification) through to Level 5 (Integrated System Testing). This role is designed to support your progression from a graduate engineer to an independent commissioning professional. Travel to project sites across Europe for extended periods is a core requirement of this position. **Responsibilities** * Support electrical commissioning activities across Data Centre projects under the guidance of experienced commissioning personnel. * Participate in electrical design reviews and assist in identifying and documenting design discrepancies. * Review project specifications, control philosophies, System of Operation (SOO), Description of Operation (DOO), and equipment submittals. * Read and interpret electrical drawings, including single\-line diagrams, schematics, and layouts. * Assist in the development and review of commissioning test scripts and procedures. * Witness and support Level 2 to Level 5 commissioning activities, including Installation Verification Checks (IVC), Pre\-Functional Checks (PFC), Functional Performance Tests (FPT), and Integrated System Tests (IST). * Execute assigned commissioning activities once trained and authorised by the Electrical Lead. * Accurately record test results, field observations, and commissioning documentation. * Assist with preparation of commissioning reports, trackers, dashboards, and As\-Left documentation. * Attend project meetings and provide status updates on assigned activities. * Support the review of commissioning documentation and document management systems. * Any other duties or responsibilities that may be assigned to you in your role. **Skills Required** * Strong interest in electrical engineering and commissioning within mission\-critical environments. * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. * Willingness to learn and develop technical knowledge through training and practical experience. * Ability to travel extensively across Europe for project assignments. * Commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality. **Education / Licenses / Certifications** * Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related engineering discipline. * Recent graduates or candidates with relevant internship/work placement experience are encouraged to apply. * Full driving licence preferred. * Proof of education, licenses, and certifications will be required where applicable **Benefits** * VHI Healthcare Cover * Life Assurance * Pension Contribution * Training And Educational Assistance * Annual GP Check\-Up * Extra Annual Leave * Healthcare Screening * Recognition Rewards *LotusWorks is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered.*

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: **Description** Designer Group is seeking **Graduate Electrical Engineers** to join our growing teams, with **multiple positions available for immediate start** . This is an excellent opportunity for recent Electrical Engineering graduates who want to build a career with a leading international mechanical and electrical contractor. You will gain hands\-on experience across live projects, working alongside experienced engineers, project teams, commercial teams and site leadership. As a Graduate Electrical Engineer, you will support the delivery of electrical building services across a range of projects, which may include commercial, industrial, pharmaceutical, data centre, residential, energy and infrastructure environments. You will be exposed to both office\-based and site\-based work, giving you a strong practical understanding of how electrical systems are designed, coordinated, installed, tested and commissioned. Designer Group’s graduate programme is designed to support early\-career professionals through practical project experience, mentoring, structured development and exposure to major engineering projects across Ireland, the UK and Europe. The existing graduate programme is positioned as a two\-year programme providing hands\-on experience across core teams and project environments. **Key Responsibilities Will Include** * Supporting project teams with the delivery of electrical services from early\-stage planning through to installation, testing and commissioning. * Assisting with technical reviews, drawings, specifications, submittals and project documentation. * Working with engineers, supervisors, subcontractors and site teams to support safe and efficient project delivery. * Supporting quality, safety and compliance requirements across electrical works. * Assisting with progress tracking, reporting, materials coordination and issue resolution. * Developing practical knowledge of M\&E contracting, construction sequencing, programme requirements and client expectations. * Building your technical understanding of electrical systems, site coordination, project delivery and engineering best practice. * Participating in training, mentoring and continuous professional development as part of your graduate journey. **Requirements** **Minimum requirements:** * Level 7 or Level 8 qualification in Electrical Engineering, Building Services Engineering, Energy Engineering or a related discipline. * Strong interest in electrical building services, M\&E contracting and construction project delivery. * Good communication, organisation and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work well as part of a team and take direction from experienced engineers and project leaders. * Proactive attitude, strong work ethic and willingness to learn. * Comfortable working in both office and site\-based environments. * Available to start immediately or at short notice. **Preferred But Not Essential** * Previous placement, internship or site experience within construction, M\&E, building services, utilities, energy, pharma, commercial or data centre environments. * Basic understanding of electrical drawings, schematics, technical specifications or construction documentation. * Exposure to AutoCAD, Revit, BIM, Microsoft Project, Excel or other engineering/project tools. * Interest in progressing toward professional development, CPD and chartership. **Benefits** At Designer Group, our people are central to our continued growth. We invest in graduates through structured development, mentoring and exposure to real project delivery from the beginning of their careers. **Benefits Include** * Full\-time permanent employment. * Structured graduate development. * Mentoring from experienced engineering professionals. * Exposure to major M\&E projects. * Pathway to chartership. * CPD training, including Engineers Ireland\-accredited development. * International project opportunities. * Competitive salary. * Pension plan. * Health plan. * Life cover. * Travel and subsistence allowances where applicable. * Bike to Work scheme. * Social committee and employee events. * Third\-level education grant support. Designer Group is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from suitably qualified candidates.

title: Graduate Geo-Environmental Consultant (Dublin)
description: **The Vacancy** **RSK Ireland** part of **RSK Group** offers a wide range environmental consultancy services advice to provide strategic management of both brownfield and greenfield sites. With offices in Belfast, Cork and Dublin, the company offers customers an integrated service across the island of Ireland. RSK Ireland Geo \- Environmental Team are now hiring for a **Graduate Geo\-Environmental Consultant** to join the team in **Dublin** . This is an excellent opportunity to work alongside a highly experienced team and a chance to get involved with a vast array of projects ranging from large scale brownfield site development, landfill investigations, contaminated site investigations, renewable energy projects and hydrogeology specific projects. **Key Responsibilities:** * Undertaking Preliminary Risk Assessments / Preliminary site Assessments (Phase 1 Desk Studies) and intrusive site investigations for environmental purposes * Soil and rock logging to current standards * Groundwater and surface water sampling * Managing fieldwork activities and subcontractors * Supporting and co\-ordinating a variety of projects * Preparing health and safety documentation, booking subcontractors and scheduling soil and water samples for analytical and geotechnical testing **Person Specification:** * BSc degree level qualified in Geology or a related Environmental subject * MSc in Engineering Geology/Engineering or Contaminated Land related subjects preferable but not essential * Previous site experience would be an advantage. * Interest in site investigations techniques, basic contaminated land assessment. * Understanding of laboratory testing and interpreting results with working knowledge of geotechnical and environmental guidance. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Prepared to work as part of a team or as an individual * Candidates must be willing to spend a significant amount of their working day out on site and away from home. * Full driving licence and access to a car. **Salary \& Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Continual professional development. Both internally and sponsored by RSK where required / relevant. * A flexible benefits programme including the option to buy additional holidays \#GraduateGeoenvironmental \#Geology \#Geoscience **RSK Group is an equal opportunities employer.** **The Company** RSK is a global leader in sustainable solutions focusing on environmental, engineering, and technical services. Working together, we address societal challenges aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, offering integrated solutions across sectors such as water, energy, food, infrastructure, cities, mining, and waste management. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. By providing workplace adjustments, we make it easier for people to confidently explore and pursue our career opportunities and recognise that flexibility is key to fostering a dynamic and thriving workplace. Many of our office\-based positions benefit from a flexible working arrangement, allowing our team members to achieve a balance between professional and personal commitments. We actively encourage applications from candidates of all genders, races, ethnicities, ages, neurotypes, religions or beliefs, sexual orientations, and those with disabilities. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: HR & Business Operations Graduate
description: **Protex AI is the AI Safety and Operations Intelligence Company.** At Protex AI, we are at the forefront of AI\-driven computer vision, building a safer, smarter industrial workplace with an intelligent operating system that redefines how facilities operate. Backed by top\-tier global investors, we recently secured a $36 million Series B to accelerate our mission. Industry leaders like DHL, Amazon, and Tesla trust Protex AI to drive measurable safety improvements, achieving an average 64% risk reduction within just three months of deployment. Operating in 20\+ countries, Protex is the go\-to safety partner for Fortune 500 manufacturing and logistics enterprises, transforming workplace safety with real\-time, AI\-powered insights. This is not a typical graduate role. As we scale globally, you will sit at the very centre of the business , working across People, Operations, Facilities, Finance, and Legal to keep a fast\-moving, international company running smoothly. You will support teams and stakeholders across multiple geographies, helping to build and maintain the operational foundations that allow a growing global company to move quickly and effectively. No two days will look the same, and that is exactly the point. We are a company that embraces technology and AI as a core part of how we operate. You will be expected to use AI tools confidently, whether drafting documents, researching suppliers or automating repetitive tasks. If you already experiment with AI in your daily life, you will fit right in. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **Key Responsibilities** * PEOPLE \& HR SUPPORT * Support the People team with onboarding coordination — contracts, starter packs, and IT setup requests — across multiple regions * Help maintain accurate employee records, org charts, and HR documentation as the company scales * Assist with scheduling and logistics for interviews, performance review cycles, and team events globally * Support the coordination of employee engagement initiatives, company communications , surveys, and culture programmes * Act as a first point of contact for general People queries, escalating where appropriate * BUSINESS OPERATIONS * Support the smooth day\-to\-day running of business operations across a growing, international organisation * Help maintain operational trackers, dashboards, and process documentation as we scale into new markets * Assist with cross\-functional projects — from research and data gathering to drafting updates and presentations * Use AI tools proactively to streamline workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and improve how we work * Manage internal communications and follow\-ups to ensure actions are captured and progressed * OFFICE MANAGEMENT \& FACILITIES * Oversee the day\-to\-day running of the office environment — supplies, equipment, and vendor relationships * Act as the primary point of contact for facilities queries, maintenance requests, and health \& safety compliance * Manage meeting room bookings, visitor coordination, and office access * Support planning and logistics for company events, offsites, and global town halls * Own the relationship with building management and key service providers * FINANCE SUPPORT * Assist with purchase order (PO) creation, invoice processing, and expense management * Support month\-end processes including cost tracking and basic budget reconciliation * Help maintain financial records and filing in line with company policy * Liaise with the Finance team on approvals and payment queries from internal stakeholders across regions * CO\-FOUNDER SUPPORT * Provide diary management support for the Co\-Founders — scheduling meetings, coordinating international travel where required * Handle ad hoc personal and business requests with discretion and professionalism ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **THE GRADUATE WE'RE LOOKING FOR** **Essential** * Degree\-educated (any discipline), with a strong academic record or demonstrable aptitude * Genuinely enthusiastic — brings positive energy and curiosity every single day * Comfortable with technology and actively uses AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, or similar) in everyday work or life * Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail — nothing falls through the cracks * A natural multi\-tasker who is comfortable switching between very different priorities at pace * Strong written and verbal communication skills — clear, concise, and professional across cultures * Proactive and self\-motivated — identifies what needs doing and gets on with it * Discreet and trustworthy when handling sensitive or confidential information * Comfortable with ambiguity; thrives when given autonomy rather than a rigid brief * Fast learner who asks good questions and is not afraid to figure things out DESIRABLE * Previous internship or work experience in a fast\-growth startup, professional services, or operations environment * Exposure to HR systems (e.g. BambooHR, Rippling, HiBob) or project management tools (e.g. Notion, Asana, Monday.com) * Has built personal workflows or automations using AI tools (e.g. Zapier, Make, or AI\-assisted drafting) * Interest in finance, operations, or people functions as a long\-term career pathway * Experience working in or supporting an international or multi\-site organisation * Experience providing administrative or coordination support to senior stakeholders **Tech \& Ai Comfort — What We Mean** You don't need to be a developer. But you should be genuinely curious about technology and comfortable picking up new tools quickly. Familiarity with AI tools is a strong plus — and we will support you in building expertise further. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **What We Offer** * Compensation package of €35,000 per year, as well as benefits * A front\-row seat at a fast\-growing global company — your work will matter from day one * Real breadth of exposure across People, Operations, Finance, Legal, and the Founders * The chance to help build and shape operational processes as we scale internationally * Direct access to experienced founders and senior leadership * Competitive graduate salary with clear progression pathways * A culture that invests in AI tools, learning, and doing things smarter * Hybrid working and a vibrant, collaborative office environment * Regular company events, team socials, and a strong people\-first culture ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **KEY COMPETENCIES** Candidates will be assessed against the following core competencies throughout the hiring process: Adaptability \& Flexibility Comfortable moving between very different tasks — from diary management to cross\-border compliance filing — with equal care and quality. Stakeholder Management Builds positive relationships at all levels and across cultures, from office suppliers to Co\-Founders, and communicates effectively in all directions. Ownership \& Initiative Takes responsibility for outcomes rather than waiting to be directed. Proactively flags risks and proposes solutions. Rigour \& Accuracy Produces accurate, well\-organised work consistently — especially critical when supporting finance, legal, and people functions across geographies. Discretion \& Integrity Handles confidential information with maturity and sound judgement at all times. Drive \& Ambition Brings energy, enthusiasm, and a genuine desire to grow. Sees this role as a launchpad and is motivated to develop across multiple disciplines in a global environment. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **SOUND LIKE YOU?** This role is deliberately broad — and the right person will be energised by that, not daunted. We want a bright, enthusiastic graduate who loves technology, moves fast, and takes real pride in making things run smoothly across a growing global business. Someone who is just as happy sorting out the office as they are chasing down a contract signature, or helping onboard a new joiner in another country. If that sounds like you — we'd love to hear from you. Protex AI is an inclusive and equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating an equitable workplace for everyone regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race.

title: HR Operations Lifecycle Specialist - EMEA - Ireland
description: **Responsibilities** The HR operations team, endeavours to continuously build and deliver a seamless and positive employee experience across the whole employee lifecycle. We are dedicated to leading with care and have empathy in mind. We strive to develop efficient and simple people processes, systems, policies, and programs. We aspire to ensure employees feel heard and that they are given the appropriate resources and support needed to be effective and efficient during their careers. Responsibilities: 1\. Ensure legal compliance by managing and implementing applicable human resource local requirements for the local market in Ireland, with the potential of extended remit to additional countries within Europe. 2\. Be responsible for vendor management to improve operation and service quality, efficiency and experience (e.g. benefits vendors). 3\. Committed to building good and harmonious employee relations. 4\. First point of contact for any employee queries. Support employees and improve employee experience by providing end\-to\-end HR solutions to solve their issues. 5\. Design and recommend enhancements, improvements and simplifications of existing HR processes and procedures. 6\. Support employee life cycle management processes such as on\-boarding management, termination, personal data changes, internal transfers , leave management \& benefit operations including relevant documentation and employee file management in accordance to local legislation. 7\. Support relocation, immigration and other responsibilities related to staffing administration. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: 1\. Relevant training/degree and experience within People Operations/Human Resources. 2\. Strong knowledge of UK \& Ireland employment legislation/ compliance laws and market practices and favourably additional knowledge of other countries. 3\. Experience with employee lifecycle (onboarding, transfers, performance cycles, leaves of absence and off\-boarding) and providing end to end HR solutions. 4\. Analytical and problem\-solving skills. 5\. High level of discretion, confidentiality, and ethics. 6\. Humility, flexibility, and good interpersonal \& customer facing skills are a must. 7\. Excellent communication skills. Preferred Qualifications: 1\. Strong knowledge of multiple legislations and market practices within the region. **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​
