title: Online Fulfilment Warehouse Operative
description: **Online Fulfilment Warehouse Operative** ----------------------------------------- **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It's the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don't just embrace our differences \- we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are \- and that's something we are really proud of. Which is why we've summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. We currently have an exciting opportunity for an **Online Fulfilment Warehouse Operative** to join our team in our **Ballymun** Warehouse. **This is a temporary 6 month contract.** ***The care you take here, gets care to patients everywhere.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us** We've got your back: employee assistance programme Perks you'll actually use: Generous in store discounts Belonging at Work: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you're valued for *you*. Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay The Online Fulfilment Operative is responsible for accurately picking, packing, and dispatching customer orders for online sales. The role ensures orders are processed efficiently, safely, and to a high standard to support excellent customer service and timely delivery. **Key Responsibilities** * Pick customer orders from online warehouse locations using handheld scanners. * Pack items securely and correctly to ensure safe delivery. * Prepare orders for dispatch and ensure correct labelling and documentation. * Maintain high levels of accuracy and productivity when processing orders. * Receive and store incoming stock in the correct warehouse locations. * Assist with stock control activities, including cycle counts and stocktakes. * Ensure work areas are kept clean, organised, and safe. * Follow all health and safety procedures and company policies. * Work collaboratively with team members to meet daily fulfilment targets. * Report any stock discrepancies, damaged goods, or operational issues to supervisors. **Skills and Experience** * Previous warehouse or order fulfilment experience preferred but not essential. * Good attention to detail and accuracy when handling orders. * Ability to work efficiently in a fast\-paced environment. * Basic computer or handheld scanner skills. * Good communication and teamwork skills. * Ability to follow processes and instructions. **Physical Requirements** * Ability to stand and walk for extended periods. * Ability to lift and move boxes (up to 20kg where required). * Comfortable working in a warehouse environment. **Desirable Attributes** * Experience working with stock management systems * Forklift or manual handling certification * Flexible availability for peak trading periods. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland's leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland's leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe's leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@mccabespharmacy.ie***

title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. That’s the real meaning of studio\-to\-street: taking the consciousness from practice on the mat and putting it into practice in life. **Objective** The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving to profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. **This is a 6\-month, fixed\-term contract.** **Key Job Responsibilities** * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead. * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; including but not limited to visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence * Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business\-driving strategies * Resolve client needs quickly \& effectively ensuring customer satisfaction * Identify product concerns and communicate inventory needs to support the business goals * Support other areas of the business, including Sales \& Service and Cashier, as needed **Operations Associate Qualifications** * Preferred prior work experience in a client\-centric, sales \& operational environment * Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences * High energy, upbeat and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work * Self\-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually, and as a team * Fosters genuine connection through empathy, compassion, integrity and building trusting relationships * Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles * Proof of right to live and work in the UK **Operations Associate Schedule** Part\-Time associates are expected to work up to 29 hours per week; Full\-Time associates are expected to work 32\-40 hours per week with 100% of their work week in operations and floor support. All employees' availability must suit the needs of the business, which are subject to change. The General Manager determines availability needs and creates a schedule including morning, evening, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change. All Employees are expected to be able to work from the week of and including “Black Friday” until the end of the holiday season, first week of January. **Diversity and Inclusion statement** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, ALO does not discriminate against applicants or employees because of race, colour, creed, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, age, citizenship, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression or because of any other status or condition protected by statutory law. We are committed to an active Inclusion, Diversity and Equal Opportunities Policy, which starts with our recruitment and selection process, and we are happy to talk flexible working. **Interview Process adjustments** If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application or by emailing europehr@aloyoga.com . We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. For CA residents, Job Applicant Privacy Policy HERE.

title: PLATFORM SUPPORT ENGINEER (450)
description: Réalta Technologies are searching for a Platform Support Engineer to support our Pharmaceutical client based in Dublin. This candidate will provide technical ownership and ongoing support for manufacturing digital platforms within a regulated pharmaceutical environment. The successful candidate will act as a key interface between Automation, MES, IT and Operations teams to ensure reliable integration, performance and continuous improvement of site manufacturing systems. The role will support the full system lifecycle including platform administration, equipment integration, change management, qualification activities and troubleshooting of manufacturing information systems. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide technical support and ownership for manufacturing platforms including MES, OPC interfaces, industrial data historians and integrated automation systems. * Support the integration of process equipment and control systems with MES and site digital platforms using OPC and related technologies. * Configure, maintain and troubleshoot MES interfaces, data collection systems and historian solutions such as AVEVA PI. * Lead and support change control activities, including impact assessments, implementation planning and documentation updates. * Develop, execute and review qualification documentation, including test protocols, commissioning activities and validation deliverables in accordance with GMP requirements. * Investigate and resolve platform issues, performing root cause analysis and implementing sustainable corrective actions to ensure system reliability. * Collaborate with Automation, CSV, IT, Engineering and Operations teams to support new equipment integrations, upgrades and site projects. * Support the lifecycle management of manufacturing systems, including patching, upgrades, periodic reviews and obsolescence planning. * Maintain system documentation, including design specifications, configuration records, SOPs and support procedures. * Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance data integrity, system performance and manufacturing efficiency. **Job Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in Automation, Electrical, Computer, Chemical Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience supporting manufacturing, automation, or digital platforms within a pharmaceutical or other GMP\-regulated environment. * Experience supporting and integrating Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) with plant equipment and control systems. * Strong understanding of industrial communication protocols, particularly OPC technologies. * Hands\-on experience with industrial data historians, preferably AVEVA PI. * Knowledge of GMP requirements, computerized system lifecycle management, and change control processes. * Experience authoring and executing qualification and validation documentation, including IQ/OQ protocols and testing activities. * Familiarity with PLC, HMI, and SCADA technologies, with exposure to platforms such as Allen\-Bradley considered advantageous. * Strong troubleshooting, problem\-solving, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work effectively across Automation, IT, Validation, Engineering, and Operations teams.

title: PMO & Automation Analyst - Technology (IT PMO)
description: **Join us** ----------- At Vodafone, we’re not just shaping the future of connectivity for our customers — we’re shaping the future for everyone who joins our team. When you work with us, you’re part of a global mission to connect people, solve complex challenges, and create a sustainable and more inclusive world. If you want to grow your career whilst finding the right balance between work and life, Vodafone offers the opportunity to help you belong and make a real impact. **Role Summary** ---------------- As a **PMO \& Automation Analyst**, you will lead the transformation of project governance processes through automation and data\-driven insights. You will work closely with Project Managers, Finance, and our offshore VOIS teams to implement AI\-driven workflows, Power Automate solutions, and interactive dashboards that streamline project financial management and reporting. Your role is to reduce manual effort and improve efficiency by replacing traditional administrative tasks with smart, automated processes. This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, tech\-savvy PMO professional with a passion for innovation, data, and continuous improvement within a dynamic enterprise environment. **What You Will Do** -------------------- **Key Responsibilities:\-** **Design \& Manage Automated Workflows:** Develop and maintain automated processes (using tools like Power Automate and other AI/ML technologies) for key project governance activities, such as resource time tracking, approvals, and financial updates – ensuring these workflows run smoothly and minimize manual intervention. **Financial Governance \& Budget Tracking:** Partner with Project Managers and Finance to maintain accurate, real\-time project budgets and forecasts. Use digital tools and dashboards to track spend, forecast costs, and ensure resources are allocated efficiently. **Offshore (VOIS) Budget Coordination:** Coordinate with offshore/VOIS teams to manage and track budgets for outsourced project components. Ensure seamless integration of offshore costs into overall project financials and that all expenses are properly approved and documented. **Dynamic Reporting \& Dashboards:** Create and refine interactive dashboards and management reports (e.g., in Power BI or similar tools) that give senior stakeholders clear, real\-time visibility of project status, risks, and key metrics. Continuously improve these reporting outputs using feedback and new data techniques. **Advanced Analytics \& Insights:** Perform ad\-hoc data analysis on project and portfolio data to identify trends, anomalies, or improvement opportunities. Present insights through high\-quality visuals and presentations (e.g., slides, charts) to support decision\-making by leadership. **Governance \& Standards Ownership:** Drive consistency and best practices across the project management community by developing and promoting standard templates, automated processes, and data governance standards. Ensure that project teams adhere to these standards for efficient, aligned ways of working. **Continuous Improvement \& Innovation:** Proactively identify process inefficiencies or manual work steps in project and financial management. Conceive and implement improvements – from small process tweaks to larger automation initiatives – that increase productivity and reduce errors. **Stakeholder Engagement \& Training:** Act as a champion for new tools and processes, coaching and supporting project teams in adopting automated solutions. Provide training or guidance to colleagues on using new dashboards and workflow tools, building a culture of data\-driven decision making. **What You’ll Need** -------------------- **Skills \& Experience:** **Technical Proficiency:** Hands\-on experience with (or strong aptitude to learn) automation and analytics tools such as Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, or similar workflow/BI platforms. Ability to design simple automation scripts or use AI\-based tools to streamline processes. **Financial \& Analytical Skills:** Solid understanding of project budgeting, forecasting, and cost management principles. Comfortable working with financial data and large datasets, demonstrating strong analysis and numeracy skills with high attention to detail and accuracy. **Project \& Tool Knowledge:** Familiarity with project management processes and common tools (e.g., SharePoint, Microsoft Project or similar PM software). Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite, especially Excel for data manipulation and PowerPoint for presentation of complex information. **Problem\-Solving \& Innovation:** A proactive, curious mindset with proven ability to identify process gaps and implement creative technical solutions. Willingness to continuously learn and experiment with new technologies (AI, bots, data analytics) to improve ways of working. **Self\-Management \& Collaboration:** Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities to deliver high\-quality outputs with minimal supervision. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills – able to work effectively with cross\-functional teams, explain technical concepts in simple terms, and influence stakeholders to embrace new processes. **Desirable** ------------- **PMO/Project Experience:** Previous experience in a Project Management Office, IT project delivery, or digital transformation support role, especially within a large or complex organisation. **Automation Credentials:** Exposure to robotic process automation (RPA), simple scripting (e.g., VBA, Python), or AI\-driven applications in a business context. Any relevant certification in Microsoft Power Platform or data analytics would be an advantage. **Global/Offshore Collaboration:** Experience working with offshore or remote teams and an understanding of cultural dynamics in a global organisation. **Industry Knowledge:** Familiarity with telecommunications or technology projects and understanding of their common challenges. **What We’re Looking For:** --------------------------- **A Tech\-Enthusiastic Problem Solver:** A motivated, detail\-oriented individual who gets excited about leveraging technology and data to drive efficiency and improve processes. **A Collaborative Influencer:** Someone who enjoys engaging with diverse stakeholders (from Project Managers to senior leaders and offshore colleagues) and can influence others to adopt new tools and practices through clear communication and demonstration of value. **A Change Champion:** A team player who embraces innovation and values governance, balancing consistency with continuous improvement. You are not afraid to challenge the status quo and can navigate change positively, acting as a champion for new ways of working. **A Growth Mindset:** An individual keen to develop their skills in automation, data analysis, and project governance within a large, dynamic enterprise. You thrive on learning new technologies and have the adaptability to evolve with the role as the organisation continues its digital transformation. **Not a perfect fit?** ---------------------- Worried that you don’t meet all the desired criteria exactly? At Vodafone we are passionate about empowering people and creating a workplace where everyone can thrive, whatever their personal or professional background. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align exactly with every part of the job description, we encourage you to still apply — you may be the right candidate for this role or another opportunity. **What’s in it for you?** ------------------------- * The flexibility to work how you perform best, with hybrid ways of working and real focus on work\-life balance * A competitive package including salary, bonus and a strong pension to support you now and long\-term * ''Generous'' flexible benefits allowance, plus access to comprehensive health and wellbeing support * Access to our on\-site wellness centre and gym, alongside a wider wellbeing programme * Free mobile plan, device offers and exclusive discounts for you, your family and friends * Market\-leading family policies and flexibility to support you at every stage of life * Real opportunities for career growth through learning, development and internal progression **Who we are** -------------- We are a leading international Telco, serving millions of customers. At Vodafone, we believe that connectivity is a force for good. If we use it for the things that really matter, it can improve people’s lives and the world around us. Through our technology, we empower people — connecting everyone regardless of who they are or where they live — and we protect the planet, whilst helping our customers do the same. Belonging at Vodafone isn’t a concept; it’s lived, breathed, and cultivated through everything we do. You’ll be part of a global and diverse community with many different minds, abilities, backgrounds and cultures. We’re committed to increasing diversity, ensuring equal representation, and making Vodafone a place where everyone feels safe, valued and included. Vodafone is committed to supporting candidates with disabilities during the recruitment process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know. **Together we can.** **\#LI\-JB1**

title: Part-Time Mobile Patrol Security Officer
description: **PART\-TIME MOBILE PATROL SECURITY OFFICER** Weekend Day Shifts (Saturday \& Sunday) **Dublin, Kildare, Laois \& Offaly** CSC Covert Security Consultants Limited is an established Irish total security solutions provider, delivering manned guarding, mobile patrol, alarm response and electronic security services nationwide since 2007\. We are currently recruiting a reliable, professional Mobile Patrol Security Officer to join our team on a part\-time basis, covering Saturday and Sunday day shifts across the Dublin, Kildare, Laois and Offaly region. **The Role** * Carrying out scheduled mobile patrols of client sites across the region * Conducting site checks, lock\-ups / unlocks and key\-holding duties * Responding to alarm activations and reporting incidents promptly * Completing accurate patrol logs and reports * Acting as a professional, visible representation of CSC and our clients **What We’re Looking For** * A valid PSA licence (Security Officer / Door Supervisor) – essential * A full, clean Irish driving licence – ***essential*** * Strong reliability, punctuality and attention to detail * Good written and verbal communication skills * The ability to work independently and use initiative * Previous mobile patrol or security experience an advantage **What We Offer** * €16\.10 per hour – above the industry Employment Regulation Order (ERO) minimum rate * Consistent weekend day\-shift hours, ideal for supplementing income or fitting around other commitments * Full support from an experienced operations team * The opportunity to join a growing, professional Irish security company **How to Apply** To apply, send your CV to lloyd@cscsecurity.ie or call 0818 273 274\. **Please note: you must be currently living in Ireland and hold a valid right to work in Ireland to apply. You must also hold a current PSA licence. Applications from candidates not resident in Ireland cannot be considered.** Pay: €16\.10 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Partners - New Advisory Business Lines
description: **Job Title:** Partners – New Advisory Business Lines **Sector:** Professional Services / Financial Services **Location:** Dublin **Salary:** To be discussed **Our Client** Our client is a growing professional services firm in Dublin, with a strong reputation across core service lines including Audit, Tax, Corporate Finance, Risk \& Internal Audit, Sustainability, and Outsourced Accounting. With ambitious growth plans and a collaborative, forward\-thinking leadership team, they are now seeking to expand into **new service lines** that complement and enhance their existing capabilities. **Why Should You Apply?** This is a rare opportunity for entrepreneurial senior professionals who are currently constrained by traditional partnership structures or a lack of support for innovation in their current firms. Areas of strong interest include (but are not limited to): * **Technology Risk \& Cybersecurity Advisory** * **Data \& Analytics Consulting** * **Aircraft Leasing Advisory** * **Fund Services / Asset Management Advisory** * **Insurance Advisory \& Finance Transformation** * **Finance Transformation \& Automation** If your idea aligns with the evolving needs of financial services clients, whether regulatory, operational, digital or strategic, this could be the perfect platform to bring it to life. **Who Should Apply?** We’re keen to speak with experienced professionals who are: * Currently operating at Director or Partner level in a professional services or consultancy environment * Commercially minded with a strong network and track record in client delivery and business development * Entrepreneurial, ambitious and ready to take ownership of a practice area * Seeking to build a team, shape a proposition, and leave a legacy **Interested in this position?** To apply, please submit your CV to **Mark Baker** who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further, please contact Mark directly on 01 529 4201\.

title: Performance Tester
description: **Job Description – Performance Tester (JMeter / BlazeMeter)** We are looking for an experienced **Performance Tester** with strong expertise in performance testing tools like **JMeter and BlazeMeter** . The ideal candidate should have hands\-on experience in designing, executing, and analyzing performance test scenarios for web, API, and enterprise applications. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design and execute performance test plans, test scenarios, and scripts based on business and technical requirements. * Develop and maintain performance test scripts using **Apache JMeter** and **BlazeMeter** . * Perform different types of performance testing including load, stress, spike, endurance, and scalability testing. * Analyze performance test results and identify bottlenecks across applications, APIs, databases, and infrastructure. * Generate performance reports with key metrics, findings, and recommendations. * Collaborate with development, QA, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to troubleshoot performance issues. * Monitor application behavior during performance tests and provide tuning recommendations. * Integrate performance testing into CI/CD pipelines where required. **Required Skills:** * Strong hands\-on experience in **Performance Testing** . * Expertise in **JMeter** scripting, execution, and result analysis. * Experience with **BlazeMeter** for performance test execution and reporting. * Strong understanding of HTTP/HTTPS protocols, REST APIs, and web application performance testing. * Experience with performance monitoring and analysis tools. * Good knowledge of SQL and database performance analysis. * Understanding of Agile methodology and defect management processes. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills. **Good to Have:** * Experience with cloud\-based performance testing environments. * Knowledge of CI/CD tools (Jenkins/Git\-based pipelines). * Experience with Java scripting or Groovy for JMeter enhancements. * Experience testing large\-scale enterprise applications.

title: Plant Operator I
description: **About Us** Stericycle is a U.S. based business\-to\-business services company and leading provider of compliance\-based solutions that protects people and brands, promotes health and well\-being, and safeguards the environment. Since our founding over 30 years ago, we have grown from a small start\-up in medical waste management into a leader across a range of increasingly complex and highly regulated arenas, serving healthcare organizations and commercial businesses of every size. Every day, we help our customers solve complex challenges by safely managing materials that could otherwise spread disease, contaminate the environment, or compromise one’s identity. Join us on our mission to protect health and well\-being in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way. **Position Purpose** The Plant Operative is responsible for the receipt, management, and transfer out of all waste in compliance with licence conditions and company procedures. **Key Job Activities** To receive, manage and transfer out all waste within the facility. Ensure all paperwork is completed in line with Company procedure and legislation To work in all areas of the facility. To ensure bins are clean and serviceable and carry out maintenance of clinical waste bins To ensure waste is properly segregated on arrival and dispatch to and from the facility To load bulk waste trailers to meet the needs of the facility To load and unload waste into bins and containers to meet the needs of the facility Use and operate the waste tracking systems that are used by the company To ensure the highest standards of quality are maintained. To maintain a safe working environment. To work alongside outlined Standard Operational Procedures. To observe Health \& Safety Regulations, other relevant legislation and safe working practices. To report equipment faults and defects of any nature to the site management and rectify where possible. All other duties deemed reasonable to carry out duties with the job role. **Education** **Experience (North America \& LATAM):** High School or equivalent. **Experience (EMEAA)** Preferred Experience: Basic knowledge of plant / warehouse operations. Required: Manual handling training, trained in handling chemical waste. Preferred: Forklift training. Physically fit. Excellent organisation skills and attention to detail. Good communicator. Ability to work without supervision. Ability to manage time and complete multi tasks in line with timescales. Self\-motivated, reliable and enthusiastic. Team player. Able to work effectively under pressure. Forklift certificate and warehouse experience would be advantageous. **Benefits** **Certifications and/or Licenses:** **Stericycle Offers You** * Contributory Pension Scheme * Life Insurance * Access to SteriCares, our employee support fund * Stericycle University – Our online library of self\-development \& learning * Annual performance related pay review. * Referral Scheme (Earn by introducing people in your network to the Stericycle family) * Flu voucher * Eye Test voucher And more… **Our Promise** Stericycle is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, status as a protected veteran or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. **Disclaimer** *The above description is meant to provide a summary of the nature and level of work being performed; it should not be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and requirements of the job. This document does not create an employment contract, implied or otherwise, and it does not constitute any right or guarantee of employment condition. This position is open to people with disabilities. Stericycle will consider requests for workplace accommodations for protected physical or mental limitations in accordance with its human resources and risks prevention policies and local laws. To the extent permissible under local law, and consistent with business necessity, Stericycle reserves the right to modify the content formally or informally, either verbally or in writing, at any time with or without advance notice.*

title: Platform Engineer
description: **Platform Engineer \- Contract Role Dublin ( Hybrid 3 days South Dublin)** We are building resilient, secure, and scalable cloud platforms that support next\-generation digital applications. We are seeking a Platform Engineer with strong hands\-on experience in AWS infrastructure and networking to help design, build, and support cloud\-native environments. In this role, you will be a key contributor within a cloud platform team, focusing on automation, infrastructure as code, and secure delivery pipelines. You will collaborate with engineering, security, and operations teams to deliver well\-architected solutions aligned with industry best practices. **Requirements** * 7\+ years of experience in software engineering, platform engineering, or site reliability engineering, including 5\+ years of hands\-on AWS experience. * Proven experience designing and operating production\-grade cloud infrastructure in regulated or security\-sensitive environments. * Strong understanding of cloud security, networking, identity and access management, and governance within AWS. * Advanced knowledge of networking concepts, including firewalls, DNS, load balancing, proxies, and routing. * Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language (e.g., Python, Go, or Shell). * Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and working with GitOps workflows. * Strong collaboration and communication skills. * Experience with multi\-account cloud architectures, landing zones, or cloud governance frameworks. * Familiarity with zero\-trust architecture, encryption strategies, and key management systems (e.g., KMS). * Strong experience with Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform, including building modular and reusable infrastructure components. * Experience with containerization (Docker) and orchestration platforms such as Kubernetes (e.g., EKS). * Experience with observability and monitoring tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk, Dynatrace, and AWS\-native services. * Strong ability to optimize cost, performance, and reliability across large\-scale cloud environments. * Experience in regulated industries such as financial services, fintech, or similar domains is highly desirable. * Contributions to open\-source projects or active participation in cloud engineering communities is a plus. **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to** **www.berkley\-group.com** **.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to jburke@berkley\-group.com**

title: Power Systems Engineer
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** ELSE Cluster Manager North Europe \_\_ **Your role and responsibilities** In this role you will work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment, developing technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry\-recognized consulting engineer. The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive, innovative electrical consultancy. We are seeking experienced Power Systems Engineers who are eager to deepen their technical expertise and contribute to complex engineering projects for global clients. This role is ideal for a practical, adaptable, and technically driven engineer who wants to grow into an industry\-recognized consulting specialist within a progressive and innovative electrical consultancy environment. The work model for the role is: \#hybrid, \#Li\-hybrid Driven by strong business growth and increasing customer demand, we are expanding our team and recruiting multiple professionals for this role across the listed countries. You will be mainly accountable for: * Providing technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects. * Working closely with clients to understand their technical and business requirements and translating them into practical engineering solutions. * Ensuring project outputs meet client expectations, quality standards, timeframes, and budget requirements. * Conducting in\-depth power systems analysis using industry\-leading methodologies and software tools. * Preparing detailed technical and non\-technical reports, presenting clear recommendations and actionable insights. * Attending client meetings remotely, building trusted relationships and managing expectations through effective communication and responsiveness. * Tracking and reporting on project performance and client satisfaction. * Mentoring, coaching and developing more junior engineers. Online training to be delivered where applicable. **Typical projects include:** * Power system studies include load flow, short circuit, protection coordination and system resilience assessments. * Electrical safety and power quality studies, including arc flash, harmonics and earthing assessments. * Grid connection and renewable energy integration projects, covering stability, harmonics and compliance analyses. * Reliability assessments and failure investigations for critical electrical infrastructure. * Software\-based engineering solutions and emerging technology evaluations. * Supporting wind energy projects from design through installation and commissioning. **Qualifications for the role** * You are highly skilled in power system studies, including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash analysis, and enjoy working on complex electrical infrastructure projects. * You have 3\+ years of experience in power systems engineering, applying technical expertise to deliver reliable, safe and efficient electrical solutions. * You possess advanced knowledge of industry\-leading power system analysis tools such as SKM, EasyPower, ETAP or similar. * You have proven experience in protection system design, settings development and coordination studies, with exposure to transformer and bus differential protection, REF and directional protection schemes. * You have experience performing electrical calculations and developing designs in accordance with relevant IEC standards. * Knowledge of power quality, harmonics and renewable energy integration studies would be an advantage. * You are passionate about solving technical challenges, collaborating with clients and stakeholders, and continuously expanding your expertise. * Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. * You are at ease communicating in English and can clearly present technical concepts to a wide range of audiences **Compensation Package** This opportunity is open to candidates in multiple countries, with the final work location determined based on the selected candidate. As compensation may vary by location, we are committed to being transparent throughout the hiring process and will share details of the salary range, variable compensation and benefits package with candidates who progress to the first interview stage. The offer will be tailored based on your role location, experience, skills and qualifications, internal market positioning and alignment with ABB internal standards. **Compensation Package** This opportunity is open to candidates in multiple countries, with the final work location determined based on the selected candidate. As compensation may vary by location, we are committed to being transparent throughout the hiring process and will share details of the salary range, variable compensation and benefits package with candidates who progress to the first interview stage. The offer will be tailored based on your role location, experience, skills and qualifications, internal market positioning and alignment with ABB internal standards. Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Powertrain Engine Diagnostician & Repair Technician
description: Transport is at the core of modern society. Imagine using your expertise to shape sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions for the future. If you seek to make a difference on a global scale, working with next\-gen technologies and the sharpest collaborative teams, then we could be a perfect match. **Main Mission** The Engine Diagnostician \& Repair Technician is responsible for performing all rework operations at the Complete Vehicle Assembly Plant (CVA). Including assembly, disassembly, diagnosis, analysis, and adjustment of mechanical, electrical, or electronic components (parts or sub\-assemblies) on engines. While adhering to instructions regarding safety, ergonomics, environment, quality, deadlines, cost, and internal operating procedures. They must diagnose and report any non\-conformities and malfunctions found to the Powertrain Plant (HAG). **Requirements** * High school diploma preferred or completion of vocational/ technical school. * Advanced mechanical aptitude with experience preferred but not required. * Valid driver license with the ability to obtain CDL license. **Specific Professional Knowledge** * Knowledge of and adherence to all safety instructions specific to the products. * Basic and/or advanced knowledge of fault diagnosis and assembly: * Knowledge of engines, parts usage, and the assembly line. * Basic and/or advanced knowledge of electricity and electronics. * Ability to read blueprints and identify part non\-conformities (using a diagnostic tool, etc.). * Staying informed of product/process developments (monitoring product updates via the PMO or Production Quality, etc.). * Proficiency in standard office software and industry\-specific IT tools (GASS, MONT, OCD, etc.). * Ability to use assembly and measurement equipment (overhead cranes, hoists, stackers, forklifts, etc.). **Significant Activities \& Responsibilities** * Perform diagnostics and corrective actions for Engine and Transmissions at the CVA. * Document and communicate defects. * Communicate spare part needs. * Work with CVA to understand CVA damage. * Perform regular GEMBA with CVA production line. * Support Powertain Quality, ie. Quality Campaigns and Quality Improvements. * Maintain a perfectly organized and clean workstation. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the department (for example, by conducting KPIs, tagging, and undertaking cross\-functional projects for the department). At the Volvo Group, we strive for a clear, transparent, and straightforward compensation approach, motivating you to contribute to the company’s growth. For this position, the base pay is set at $29\.03 \- $35\.86/ hour, and where applicable, bonus eligible. The range for this role, as well as final salary offered, is determined by several factors including, but not limited to, geographic location, work\-related knowledge, certifications, skills, education, and experience. In addition to these factors, we believe in the importance of pay equity and consider internal equity of our current team members as part of any final offer. In addition to a solid package of compensation and benefits, plus you will enjoy: * Competitive medical, dental and vision insurance. * Generous paid time off. * Competitive matching retirement savings plans. * Working environment where your safety, health and wellbeing come first. * Focus on professional and personal development through Volvo Group University. * Programs that make today’s challenging reality of combining work and personal life easier. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. We value your data privacy and therefore do not accept applications via mail. **Who We Are And What We Believe In** We are committed to shaping the future landscape of efficient, safe, and sustainable transport solutions. Fulfilling our mission creates countless career opportunities for talents across the group’s leading brands and entities. Applying to this job offers you the opportunity to join **Volvo Group** . Every day, you will be working with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to be able to leave our society in better shape for the next generation. We are passionate about what we do, and we thrive on teamwork. We are almost 100,000 people united around the world by a culture of care, inclusiveness, and empowerment. **Trucks Technology \& Industrial Division** hire team players who are ready to create real customer impact. Our decentralized teams work close to our customers, with speed and autonomy, to build what they truly need. Join us to collaborate on innovative, sustainable technologies that redefine how we design, build, and deliver value. Bring your curiosity, your expertise, and your collaborative energy, and together, we’ll turn bold ideas into tangible solutions for our customers and contribute to a more sustainable tomorrow.

title: Product Designer (Platform)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions, collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love. We're looking for a Platform Product Designer to build internal systems and tools that enable us to operate at scale. You'll turn fragmented workflows into structured and scalable solutions for business\-critical areas, including AI tooling, customer support platforms, financial infrastructure, and internal back\-office systems. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Designing complex internal platforms and tools for global operations, support, compliance, and AI workflows * Architecting large\-scale systems, mapping end\-to\-end behaviours, states, and interactions across multi\-step processes and edge cases * Building scalable UI/UX patterns while balancing speed, usability, and technical constraints in internal tools * Turning ambiguous, fragmented, or highly technical requirements into clear product logic and usable interfaces * Partnering with Product Owners, Engineers, Data Scientists, and Operations Managers to unify product logic * Identifying bottlenecks in internal processes and redesigning them for scale and performance **What You'll Need** * 3\+ years of experience designing world\-class products in\-house, ideally internal tools or platform experiences * A portfolio featuring examples of complex product or systems design work * Impressive design skills across web platforms * The ability to think at a high level about product strategy and articulate design decisions * A solid understanding of technical implications in design decisions, including backend\-driven workflows * The ability to take ownership of design processes * A focus on UX and an understanding of how operational users behave * To be comfortable designing data\-heavy interfaces, including complex states, tables, and dense workflows * Knowledge of typography and grids **Nice to have** * A bachelor's or master's degree in computer science or related technical field * Programming skills and the ability to code simple prototypes * Expertise in designing and maintaining cross\-platform design systems * Experience working in a fast\-paced startup environment * Familiarity with Android and iOS Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Project Engineer - Water Networks
description: **The Opportunity** Are you a talented **Project Engineer** with a passion for water network design? We have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and motivated individual to join our team at Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec. You'll play a key role in the planning, design, and delivery of crucial water network projects, helping to shape the future of Ireland's water infrastructure. Your responsibilities will include developing detailed designs for water network infrastructure, including pumping stations and storage facilities. You will also prepare feasibility studies, site assessments, and technical evaluations, as well as create specifications and tender documents for projects. You will coordinate with clients, contractors, and our multidisciplinary teams, and you'll manage and guide others in preparing project drawings and other inputs. Throughout the entire project lifecycle, you will provide expert technical support. **About You** You will have a passion for the water sector and have the following : * A third\-level degree in engineering (Level 8 minimum). * Strong post graduate experience in water network design. * Strong professional and technical knowledge. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * The ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * A keen eye for detail and strong problem\-solving skills. * The ability to manage deadlines and juggle multiple tasks effectively. * Proficiency in industry\-standard software. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package. * Access to excellent professional growth and development opportunities. * A collaborative and supportive work environment. * Flexibility to be based in our Dublin, Galway, Cork, or Castlebar office. * Work on impactful projects that are making a real difference across Ireland. If you're ready to take on a challenging and rewarding role, we'd love to hear from you. **Interested? Apply now to join our dynamic team and help us build a better future.** **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Property Management Surveyor
description: JLL supports the Whole You, personally and professionally. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology to our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people in our industry; and we support them through professional growth, flexibility, and personalized benefits to manage life in and outside of work. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades, and technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, we empower you to shape a brighter way forward so you can thrive professionally and personally. Job DescriptionSurveyor \- Property Management Grade P2 About JLLJLL shapes the future of real estate through world\-class services, advisory, and technology. The Surveyor role in Property Management (PM) team focuses on delivering exceptional property management, contributing to the Centre of Excellence, and maintaining strong client relationships. The Role This role is a high profile Retail Management Role working a mixed portfolio, including a trophy shopping centre (Cabot Circus) for 2 key JLL clients, Hammerson and L\&G. Office location The role is based in Bristol and will sit within JLL’s Property Delivery Team. Key ResponsibilitiesClient \& Stakeholder SupportSupport the Property lead as a key contact for the Asset Manager across allocated assets. Maintain professional and responsive communication with clients, occupiers, and stakeholders. Assist in preparing client reports and attending meetings where required. Lease and Legal ManagementAssist in receiving, progressing, and preparing recommendations on occupier applications (licence to alter, wayleaves, underletting, assignments). Monitor tenant compliance with lease covenants and obligations, escalating issues and supporting resolution. Respond to routine technical queries in relation to leases and licences, referring more complex matters where appropriate. Support resolution of occupier matters that may influence asset performance or valuation. Financial ManagementSupport financial processes including raising charges, monitoring income, and liaising with accounts teams. Assist with arrears management in collaboration with credit control teams. Maintain accurate financial records in line with internal processes and client requirements. Service ChargeAssist in the preparation and management of service charge budgets, reconciliations, and cash flow forecasts. Ensure compliance with RICS guidelines and support achievement of client KPIs. ESGSupport the delivery of ESG initiatives in collaboration with FM and specialist teams. Assist in implementing client ESG strategies across the portfolio. Supplier \& Facilities ManagementSupport management of third\-party suppliers, monitoring performance and escalating issues where necessary. Work collaboratively with colleagues to ensure service delivery meets required standards. InspectionsUndertake regular property inspections, ensuring assets are well presented and issues are identified, recorded, and progressed for resolution. Transactions / TransitionsSupport property transitions, including onboarding and offboarding assets and assisting with due diligence processes. Liaise with solicitors and third parties under the direction of the Associate. Occupier Engagement \& Customer ExperienceDevelop positive relationships with occupiers and support initiatives to enhance customer satisfaction. Gather occupier intelligence and feedback for reporting to the client. Assist in the delivery of customer experience initiatives across the portfolio. WorksSupport delivery of major works and site\-based projects, working with consultants, contractors, and internal teams. Health \& SafetyMaintain an understanding of relevant Health \& Safety legislation and support compliance across properties. InsuranceAssist with the insurance renewal process and day\-to\-day insurance matters. Support administration of insurance claims through to resolution. Void Property ManagementSupport management of vacant units, ensuring compliance with client and insurer requirements. FeesAssist in capturing and tracking fee\-earning opportunities in line with agreed processes. Additional ResponsibilitiesSupport business development activities and identify opportunities to enhance client relationships. Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the team. Assist with project\-based work (internal and external). Identify and help resolve legacy property management issues. Candidate ProfileIdeally working towards MRICS or recently qualified. Solid experience in property management or related role. Good understanding of landlord and tenant matters. Strong organisational skills with attention to detail. Commercially aware with a proactive approach to problem solving. Strong communication skills and ability to build relationships. Competent IT skills, including property management systems. Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. Behavioural SkillsCollaborative team player supporting colleagues and wider team objectives. Professional and confident communicator (internal and external). Willingness to learn and develop technical expertise. Positive, proactive, and solution\-oriented mindset. Adheres to JLL working practices and values. Contributes to an inclusive and supportive team culture. If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements below. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! Personalized benefits that support personal well\-being and growth: JLL recognizes the impact that the workplace can have on your wellness, so we offer a supportive culture and comprehensive benefits package that prioritizes mental, physical and emotional health. About JLL – We’re JLL—a leading professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We have operations in over 80 countries and a workforce of over 102,000 individuals around the world who help real estate owners, occupiers and investors achieve their business ambitions. As a global Fortune 500 company, we also have an inherent responsibility to drive sustainability and corporate social responsibility. That’s why we’re committed to our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world. We’re using the most advanced technology to create rewarding opportunities, amazing spaces and sustainable real estate solutions for our clients, our people, and our communities. Our core values of teamwork, ethics and excellence are also fundamental to everything we do and we’re honored to be recognized with awards for our success by organizations both globally and locally. Creating a diverse and inclusive culture where we all feel welcomed, valued and empowered to achieve our full potential is important to who we are today and where we’re headed in the future. And we know that unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives help us think bigger, spark innovation and succeed together.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: We're expanding our team and looking for an experienced Quantity Surveyor to support our development team. If you're a self starter, with strong commercial experience, this is your opportunity to make a real impact with a renowned developer in Ireland. The candidate must be able to work from site in Sandyford Dublin a minimum of 3 days per week. There is potential to work from other office locations in Dunboyne Meath or Palmerstown House Estate (Award winning gold course) close to Naas Kildare. **Role Responsibilities** * Prepare tender documentation, evaluate subcontractor proposals, and manage negotiations through to order placement. * Oversee the financial and commercial management of subcontractors, ensuring smooth progression to final account settlement. * Maintain accurate site records and ensure robust document control processes. * Carry out site measurements and record findings diligently. * Contribute to forecasting, target setting, and reconciliation of package values. * Compile valuations and variations with comprehensive supporting details, agreeing adjustments as work advances. * Assist with reconciling proposed payment runs. * Update and submit projected final accounts promptly. * Collaborate with site management to address and challenge commercial issues effectively. * Drive the implementation of the agreed commercial strategy. * Take part in contract value reviews, maintaining an objective perspective. * Identify potential risks and opportunities, providing informed assessments. * Spearhead the project cost tracking process ensuring budgets and actual spend are aligned and maintained. * Build knowledge of insurance requirements and warranty obligations. * Support cash flow management activities to maintain healthy financial performance. * Assist with tendering processes when required. * Supervise and mentor Junior Quantity Surveyors, ensuring quality and accuracy in their work. * Ensure all site records and control documents are properly maintained. * Communicate clearly and effectively, both in writing and verbally. * Make full use of digital tools and technology platforms. * Maintain proficiency in Standard Methods of Measurement such as ARM5 where applicable depending on the package * Demonstrate strong commercial awareness and accountability. * Apply effective time management, organisational, interpersonal, and negotiation skills. **Qualifications and Requirements** * Minimum 4 years experience working as a quantity surveyor in Ireland, ideally with a developer or main contractor. * Excellent English level minimum C1 * Must be able to work from site in Sandyford Dublin a minimum of 3 days per week, with potential to work from locations in Dunboyne Meath or Palmerstown House Estate close to Naas Kildare * Degree\-level education and progression towards relevant professional accreditation. * Proficient in Microsoft excel and measurement tools such as Bluebeam / Cubit etc * Strong ability for cost analysis and separating value and risk from noise. * Skilled in negotiation and always driving for best value for Comer Group. * Able to manage multiple workflows simultaneously

description: Benefit from Dalata Academy programmes, courses and learning tools that equip you with essential skills and knowledge, while also providing exposure to senior team members who can mentor you along the way. We are committed to promoting from within, so your dedication can lead to exciting internal opportunities. If you are ready to enhance your career and make an impact in a supportive environment, join us at Dalata Hotel Group! **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **Your Job** * Greet and welcome guests as they arrive at the hotel. * Register guests and process their payments. * Respond to any inquiries guests may have about in\-house facilities and local tourist information. * Ensure the security of room keys. * Update all guest information in the computer system. * Take and confirm reservations for guests **What You'll Need** * Excellent customer service skills. * Ability to work independently and take initiative. * Outstanding communication skills. * Capability to remain calm and perform well under pressure. * Experience in 3/ 4\-star hotel is desired but not essential **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – we bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. title: Research Assistant Level 1
description: **Department:** School of Business **Vacancy ID:** 042443 **Closing Date:** 05\-Jul\-2026 We are seeking a Research Assistant to join the project “Duck\-FEED: Duckweed as a novel, sustainable source of protein for Ireland”, which is funded by the Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine, Ireland”. The Duck\-Feed project, that brings together researchers from Maynooth University (MU), Technological University Dublin (TUD), Teagasc, and University College Cork (UCC). The central objective of the Duck\-Feed project is to develop improved, small scale outdoor cultivation systems, develop Lemna growth on farm waste\-streams, optimise protein extraction technology, assess feed quality, economic and environmental viability, and public acceptance of duckweed as a native, protein\-rich feedstock. **Research Assistant (Level 1\) Salary Scale:** €34,130 p.a. **pro rata** (1 Point) Appointments will be made in accordance with public sector pay provisions. **Closing Date** 23:30hrs (local Irish time) on **Sunday 5th July 2026\.** Applications must be submitted by the closing date and time specified above. Late applications will not be accepted. **Maynooth University is an equal opportunities employer** **The position is subject to the Statutes of the University**

title: Research Associate - Energy Management & AI
description: This Job is based in Australia Research Associate \- Energy Management \& AI Apply now **Job no:** 538762 **Work type:** Full Time **Location:** Sydney, NSW, Canberra, ACT **Categories:** Post Doctoral Research Associate **Research Associate \- Engergy Management \& AI, UNSW Canberra** **Employment type:** Fixed\-term, full\-time (35 hrs per week) **Duration:** 10 months **Remuneration:** Level A from $118,467 plus 17% super **Location** : UNSW Canberra, ADFA Campus **OR** UNSW Sydney, Kensington Campus **About UNSW Canberra** University of New South Wales (UNSW) in Canberra has multiple locations in the Nation’s Capital. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment. **Pre\-Employment Checks** Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre\-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for. These pre\-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:\- * National and International Criminal history checks * Entitlement to work and ID checks * Working With Children Checks * Completion of a Gender\-Based Violence Prevention Declaration * Verification of relevant qualifications * Verification of relevant professional membership * Employment history and reference checks * Financial responsibility assessments/checks * Medical Checks and Assessments Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. **How To Apply** Make each day matter with a meaningful career at UNSW. Submit your application online before **Wednesday 8 July 2026 at 11:30pm** , including your CV and a 1–2\-page cover letter addressing the required skills and experience outlined in the position description. *In order to view the position description, please ensure that you allow pop\-ups for* *https://www.jobs.unsw.edu.au* **Get In Touch** For role\-related queries, please contact (UNSW lead): Ripon Chakrabortty \- Senior Lecturer r.chakrabortty@unsw.edu.au **For Recruitment Process\-related Queries Please Contact** Holly Whale – Talent Acquisition Consultant h.whale@unsw.edu.au *Applications cannot be accepted if sent directly to the contact listed.* Find out more about the lifestyle and benefits when working with **UNSW** For further information about UNSW Canberra, please visit: UNSW Canberra For further information on living in Canberra, please visit: Living in Canberra *UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. title: Risk Management Consulting - Infrastructure & Capital Projects
description: **Role: Risk Management Consulting \- Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** **Career Levels:** Consultant / Manager **Location:** Hybrid from Dublin or Cork, Ireland (with occasional travel) **As a team:** In the Infrastructure \& Capital Projects team, we shape how infrastructure is built and managed. We drive innovation by delivering the best\-in\-class management consultancy based on industrial experience maximised by the latest technologies. Infrastructure \& Capital Projects is one of Accenture’s biggest global growth areas, shown by recent acquisitions announced in this area, and you will be joining at a pivotal moment of growth. **About us:** We help clients deliver some of the most complex energy, transport and infrastructure programmes by fusing industry risk expertise with advanced analytics, automation and cloud platforms. From portfolio risk intelligence to project\-level decision support, our team uses AI‑augmented insight, digital twins, and interactive dashboards to improve confidence, compress decision cycles and drive better outcomes across EMEA. **The opportunity:** Join us to embed risk\-by\-design across development, design and construction—moving beyond traditional registers to predictive, data‑driven risk management. You’ll work in multidisciplinary squads with planners, commercial managers and engineers, leveraging QRA, scenario modelling and live data to inform contingency, confidence levels and executive decision\-making. At Manager level, you’ll shape portfolio‑wide risk strategies, standards and digital enablement. **Key responsibilities:** **Project \& Programme Risk Management** - Lead/support risk processes aligned to agreed frameworks and digitally enabled workflows (e.g., integrated risk platforms, automated notifications). - Facilitate structured risk workshops and drive data quality—linking risks to schedule, cost and commercial assumptions for traceability. - Track emerging risks and mitigation performance with live KPIs; escalate against tolerances using visual risk signals. **Quantitative Risk Analysis (QRA) \& Decision Support** - Deliver QRA/QSRA to inform contingency and confidence; translate qualitative risks into robust quantitative inputs and sensitivity models. - Use predictive techniques and risk driver analysis to highlight what really matters; present decision‑ready insights in interactive formats for senior stakeholders. - (Manager) Lead scenario modelling and options analysis; coach teams in QRA best practice. **Governance, Reporting \& Assurance** - Run periodic risk reviews and portfolio forums; produce executive‑level summaries showing trends, exposure changes and return‑on‑mitigation. - Ensure auditability and alignment between risk registers, cost plans, schedules and commercial baselines through integrated data models. - (Manager) Set risk standards and portfolio reporting that scales across programmes and supply chains. **Stakeholder Engagement \& Integration** - Act as a trusted advisor—embedding proportionate, value‑adding risk thinking into project and governance decisions. - Partner closely with PMs, planners, commercial managers, designers and contractors to operationalise risk insights. - (Manager) Mentor teams and uplift risk maturity across regions. **Continuous Improvement** - Analyse outcomes to identify themes; convert lessons learned into preventative controls, automation opportunities and stronger processes. - Contribute to evolving risk guidance, tools, templates and training, including data standards and dashboards. **Experience we are looking for:** **Essential:** - Proven capital project risk experience with strong facilitation skills across multi‑project environments. - Solid knowledge of qualitative and quantitative risk techniques, including supporting QRA/QSRA activities. - Ability to translate complex uncertainty into clear, actionable insight for project teams and stakeholders. - Practical experience coordinating risk workshops and contributing to risk processes, with motivation to deepen analytical capability. - Comfortable working in fast‑paced, high‑volume project settings with clear, structured communication skills. **Manager\-level:** - Leads risk reviews and QRA/QSRA delivery, presenting confidently to senior stakeholders and operating autonomously across multiple projects. - Sets and assures risk frameworks, governance processes and analytical standards across programmes or portfolios. - Coaches and uplifts team capability while shaping portfolio‑level risk strategy and providing expert challenge. **Set yourself apart** with a background in data centres or major infrastructure (energy, water, transport); exposure to estimating, scheduling and contingency; experience working on EMEA‑scale programmes. Shape risk management with next‑gen tech, access global communities of practice, gain analytics training, and advance your career across a high‑growth portfolio—while helping deliver sustainable, resilient infrastructure for the future.

title: SAP Basis Architect
description: At Sogeti, we believe the best is inside every one of us. Whether you are early in your career or at the top of your game, we’ll encourage you to fulfill your potential to be better. Through our shared passion for technology, our entrepreneurial culture , and our focus on continuous learning, we’ll provide everything you need to do your best work and become the best you can be. **YOUR ROLE** ------------- We are seeking an SAP Basis Senior Consultant / Architect to design, implement, and manage the technical architecture of SAP systems across the enterprise. This role requires deep expertise in SAP Basis administration, system performance optimization, and strategic planning for large\-scale SAP landscapes, including ECC, S/4HANA migrations and cloud deployments. **Key Responsibilities of the role:** * Define SAP technical architecture roadmap aligned with enterprise IT strategy and design high\-availability, scalable SAP landscapes for ECC, S/4HANA, BW, CRM, and other SAP components. * Oversee/execute SAP Basis activities including installation, upgrades, patches, and migrations and manage HANA database administration and performance tuning. * Drive SAP cloud migration initiatives (SAP BTP, Azure, AWS, GCP) and integrate SAP systems with middleware and third\-party applications. * Implement best practices for system monitoring, disaster recovery, and backup strategies and ensure compliance with security and regulatory standards. * Establish architecture standards and governance frameworks. * Mentor Basis teams and provide technical leadership across projects. **YOUR PROFILE** ---------------- * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field. * 10\+ years in SAP Basis administration and architecture. * Strong experience in SAP S/4HANA, HANA DB, and cloud platforms. * Expertise in SAP Basis, performance tuning, and system optimization. * Knowledge of SAP BTP, PI/PO, CPI, and integration technologies. * Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. * Proven, deep expertise of both SAP ECC and SAP 4/HANA is essential. **WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING HERE?** ---------------------------------------- * Craft your own career path through continuous learning and personal growth * Our commitment to maintain high ethical standards ensuring Diversity, Sustainability and Inclusion in our business practises * Be a part of our professional communities role framework which promotes professional growth, career development and enhances Sogeti’s capabilities across different domains Sogeti Ireland is an equal opportunity employer. We promote equality and dignity in all aspects of recruitment and employment, and all offers of employment and promotions are made on the basis of performance, competence and ability. We embrace the value of diversity within Sogeti and the entrepreneurial spirit of our people. If you’re looking to join a company with a proven track record of success and that deeply values its people, Sogeti Ireland offers the opportunity for you to grow and advance. Part of the Capgemini Group, Sogeti makes business value through technology for organizations that need to implement innovation at speed and want a local partner with global scale. With a hands\-on culture and close proximity to its clients, Sogeti implements solutions that will help organizations work faster, better, and smarter. By combining its agility and speed of implementation through a DevOps approach, Sogeti delivers innovative solutions in quality engineering, cloud and application development, all driven by AI, data and automation.
