title: Test Automation Engineer
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **Automation Test Engineer (Payments / Cards)** **Location:** Arklow office **Role Overview** We are seeking an experienced **Automation Test Engineer** to join our card payments team and **lead the design, implementation, and adoption of test automation across our platform** . This is a **hands\-on, highly technical role** requiring someone who can operate as a **self\-starter** , take ownership of the automation strategy, and build a scalable regression suite from the ground up. The successful candidate will work on a testing against a **core acquiring platform running on Stratus VOS COBOL** , introducing modern automation approaches to improve quality, speed, and confidence in releases. **Key Responsibilities** * Define and drive the automation strategy for the team, including tooling, frameworks, and best practices * Design and develop automated test frameworks to support functional, regression, and integration testing * Build a transaction simulation capability to submit ISO8583 (or similar) messages to the acquiring system * Develop and maintain a robust regression suite covering key payment flows and edge cases * Work closely with developers, QA, and business stakeholders to identify automation opportunities * Integrate automation into CI/CD pipelines where applicable * Analyse existing proof\-of\-concepts (Python/JSON/ISO8583 plugin) and evolve them into a production\-ready solution * Create test data strategies and manage test environments effectively * Document frameworks, standards, and approaches for team reuse * Mentor team members and help build automation capability within the wider QA team **Required Skills \& Experience** **Core Technical Skills** * Strong programming/scripting experience in one or more of: + Python (preferred) + Java / JavaScript or similar * Experience building test automation frameworks from scratch * Strong understanding of API\-level testing and message\-based systems * Experience working with structured data formats such as: + JSON + XML (desirable) **Payments / Domain Expertise** * Proven experience in card payments / acquiring / issuing environments * Strong understanding of: + ISO8583 messaging (essential or highly desirable) + Authorization flows and transaction lifecycles + Payment protocols and message formats * Experience working with payment simulators or transaction generators **Automation \& Tooling** * Hands\-on experience with test automation tools/frameworks * Experience building or integrating custom test harnesses/wrappers * Familiarity with queues, messaging, or host\-based integrations * Exposure to CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps, Jenkins, etc.) **Desirable Experience** * Experience with ISO8583 libraries/plugins (Python or other) * Performance or load testing experience in payments * Experience building mock services or simulators **Key Traits** * Self\-starter: able to take ambiguity and drive solutions independently * Pragmatic problem solver: comfortable working in a legacy \+ modern hybrid environment * Strong communication skills – able to bring the team along the automation journey * Delivery\-focused with a passion for improving quality and efficiency **What Success Looks Like** * A working automation framework adopted by the team * A repeatable way to submit and validate payment transactions into the acquiring system * A growing automated regression suite covering critical business flows * Improved release confidence and reduced manual testing effort * Clear documentation and approach enabling team\-wide contribution **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Trade Lifecycle Analyst
description: **Job Description** Are you ready to make a meaningful impact and advance your career with a global leader? Join a dynamic team at JPMorganChase that values collaboration, innovation, and proactive problem\-solving, helping clients navigate and capitalize on opportunities in global markets. As a Trade Lifecycle Analyst in the Custody Middle Office team, you'll ensure seamless operations and deliver exceptional client service to institutional investors, while managing complex transactions, driving strategic initiatives, and developing your expertise in a supportive, inclusive environment. **Job Responsibilities** * Monitor incoming client instructions e.g. subscription, redemption, transfer and switches * Ensure compliance as appropriate with regulatory requirements and with the relevant short, medium, and long\-term goals, objectives and values of JP Morgan * Monitor, analyse, input and process all corporate action activity on client portfolios in a manner consistent with client SLA's e.g. proxies, equalization, roll ups etc * Provide regular feedback to managers and more senior colleagues about workflow, issues etc. * Liaise with external counterparties and other departments in a competent and professional manner as representative of JP Morgan and the Custody Dept. * Assist with ad hoc projects/requests as deemed appropriate by Managers and more senior colleagues e.g. portfolio transitions, system testing etc * Handle and resolve complex/unusual operational problems that are referred from above or from Custody Client Service Group * Make sound operational decisions in those instances consistent with appropriate client service and internal Management demands. * Strive to develop hedge fund expertise demonstrating judgment and an understanding of the business. * Ensure static data maintained on system is accurate and up to date by cross referencing and validating against Funds' offering documentation, prospectuses. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills** * Bachelor's Degree required or equivalent * Self\-starter and ability to learn quickly. * Ability to work closely with business partners and interact with all staff levels. * Ability to be flexible, follow tight deadlines, organize and prioritize work. * Strategic thinking coupled with practical execution. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills including taking the initiative to drive change and enhance controls * Ability to manage through a changing business landscape. * Candidate must have a control focused agenda, and a thorough understanding of program/project risk. * Strong oral and written communication and presentation skills including the ability to communicate strategic messages clearly and consistently with the team, making linkages to individual goals and accomplishments. * Strong relationship management and partnership with a large array of constituents; ability to manage by influence to promote change. **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills** * Strong financial markets product knowledge and understanding of the transaction lifecycle in Custody and/or * Subject matter expertise in Equities, Fixed Income, FX, Collateral, Derivatives * Leadership with proven ability to manage by influence to promote change. * Experience of working on large scale, multi\-stream programs **About Us** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **About The Team** J.P. Morgan's Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: Trade Mark Associate (Trainee)
description: **The Role** The role of a Trade Mark Attorney is to assist clients with the registration and protection of their trade marks. Our team of highly experienced attorneys advise on the availability, protection, use and enforcement of trade marks. Our extensive client base ranges from start up ventures to multinational corporations in a wide range of sectors. On the job training and support will be given to enable the successful candidate to qualify as a registered Trade Mark Attorney. **The Candidate** Candidates should have a degree in Law, preferably with an IP module or a modern European language (2:1 equivalent). Applicants will have a logical approach, be capable of working both independently and as part of a team, have sharp analytical skills and excellent oral and written communication skills. In addition, applicants must possess a commitment to quality and attention to detail and also be able to work efficiently and meet deadlines.

title: Trainee Accountant
description: Build your career with Jabil! We challenge and empower you to make most of your talents, working with outstanding colleagues from diverse backgrounds who share your drive and passion to make Jabil grow! **Job Summary** Assist in the preparation of monthly accounts and quarterly forecasting. Maintenance of ledgers, and taxes for general accounting principles and reporting. General finance duties. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Compute and prepare data for journal entries, review general ledger and related financial statements for reasonableness. * Accumulate, sort and analyse information for the preparation of financial statements, general ledgers, reports and various taxes. * Assist with the various collating tasks in preparing monthly financial reports for both inside and outside the company. * Assist with quarterly forecasting. Prepare timely reports and communicate key messages with functional areas. * Adhere to all safety and health rules and regulations associated with this position and as directed by supervisor. * Comply and follow all procedures within the company security policy. **Job Qualifications** May perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned. * Third level degree or equivalent. * A commitment to complete and attain Accountancy qualification from a recognised body in Ireland. * Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions. * Advanced PC skills (excel). Prior SAP knowledge is beneficial but not essential. * Ability to write business correspondence to be read and used by all levels within Jabil Bray. * Ability to summarise and present information and respond to questions from groups of managers, clients, and potentially customers. , BE AWARE OF FRAUD: When applying for a job at Jabil you will be contacted via correspondence through our official job portal with a jabil.com e\-mail address; direct phone call from a member of the Jabil team; or direct e\-mail with a jabil.com e\-mail address. Jabil does not request payments for interviews or at any other point during the hiring process. Jabil will not ask for your personal identifying information such as a social security number, birth certificate, financial institution, driver’s license number or passport information over the phone or via e\-mail. If you believe you are a victim of identity theft, contact your local police department. Any scam job listings should be reported to whatever website it was posted in.

title: Trainee Craftsperson (Panel Beater/Spray Painter)
description: **About National Vehicle Distribution (NVD)** National Vehicle Distribution is Ireland’s leading provider of integrated vehicle logistics services. From our state\-of\-the\-art facilities, we support major automotive brands by managing the complete journey of new vehicles—from their arrival in Ireland to their final handover at dealerships. We are committed to quality, efficiency, and developing the next generation of automotive talent. The Opportunity NVD is seeking an enthusiastic and motivated Trainee Craftsperson to join our technical team. This is an excellent entry\-level opportunity to earn while you learn through a structured apprenticeship. You will work with experienced technicians on a variety of brand\-new vehicles, gaining recognised qualifications and building a solid foundation for a long\-term career in the automotive industry. Throughout your apprenticeship, you will learn essential skills in vehicle preparation, enhancement, and quality control within a high\-volume workshop environment. **Successful applicants will be invited to a recruitment day on Wednesday, 8th July at 10am in our Baldonnell Vehicle Preparation Centre.** Key Responsibilities Working under the guidance of senior craftspeople and mentors, you will: * Participate fully in all on\-the\-job training and development activities * Learn to carry out Pre\-Delivery Inspection (PDI) checks in line with manufacturer standards, including fluid levels, tyre pressures, battery health, and mechanical/electrical systems * Assist with and learn accessory installation such as tow bars, roof racks, parking sensors, and in\-car technology * Develop skills in diagnosing and resolving minor transport\-related faults and electronic issues using diagnostic tools * Learn cosmetic enhancement techniques, including minor paint touch\-ups, polishing, and interior/exterior valeting * Maintain a clean, safe, and well\-organised workshop area while learning correct tool usage and care * Complete all tasks to a high standard, following manufacturer specifications and NVD quality procedures * Accurately complete job cards and digital records for all work carried out The Ideal Candidate **Required** * A genuine interest in motor vehicles and mechanical work * A strong motivation to learn and commit to a multi\-year apprenticeship * Good manual dexterity and a logical, methodical approach to problem\-solving * Ability to follow detailed instructions and work safely * Reliable, punctual, and hardworking attitude * Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset Nice to Have * Leaving Certificate, ideally with a pass in Mathematics or a technical subject (e.g., Engineering, Technology, Technical Drawing) * Pre\-apprenticeship training in motor technology or a related discipline * Practical experience working on vehicles or machinery, even informally * Basic IT skills for using diagnostic and job management systems What We Offer * Structured apprentice pay progression * Clear career pathways, including opportunities to advance to Senior Craftsperson, Master Technician, or Workshop Management roles * Access to modern facilities, the latest diagnostic tools, and new vehicle models * Dedicated mentorship and ongoing support from highly experienced technicians * A comprehensive benefits package, including a pension scheme and employee assistance programme * All required PPE, company uniform, and a contribution towards tooling and driving lesson

title: Trainee Merchandiser
description: Because your perspective shapes trends. Innovate, your way. **Merchandising at Primark** Our merchandising team turn strategic goals into vast opportunities. Here, your insights will maximise our growth as a business and ensure we continue to stay at the forefront in the world of retail fashion. **What You’ll Do As a Trainee Merchandiser** As a Trainee Merchandiser at Primark, you’ll assist the Merchandiser and Assistant Merchandiser in the delivery of our sales, stocks and contribution budgets. You’ll learn about the industry and be given all the tools you need to progress in a Merchandiser role. Primark is a growing business in a competitive industry, with exciting expansion across the UK, Europe, and the US. Your contribution will focus on delivering budgets and line projections, investigating data inaccuracies and forecasting sales. You’ll also have exciting responsibilities relating to products and trends. **What You'll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. * Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish, and a subsidised cafeteria. * Primark Perks – Discounts with some local partner business to our offices **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Analytical \- An analytical thinker with an eye for detail and numbers, who also loves working in a team. * Curious \- Investigate and resolve data inaccuracies, report on stock overages and shortages and ensure that current commitment records have been maintained accurately. * Customer focused \- Develop a strong knowledge of our global customer base and use this knowledge to offer outstanding value to our customer, and key recommendations to your team. * Strategically minded \- Act as a self\-starter, working on a range of products trading in multiple regions. As your knowledge of your product and customer grows, you’ll identify opportunities – learning from sales reactions. * Never miss an opportunity \- Use your initiative to identify current and future trading opportunities and propose your recommendations to your team. * Total organisation \- Use your passion for people to build key relationships that will enable you to progress deliveries and shipping documentation, drive stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with your team. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility requests, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** REF3761N

title: Trainee and Chartered Accountant
description: Devaney \& Durkin is a Dublin\-based chartered accountancy and tax advisory practice with a broad client base. We advise on corporate and personal tax, company reconstructions, financial planning, investments, auditing, estate planning, forensic accounting, and cross\-border matters. We are seeking both ambitious trainees and qualified chartered accountants to join our team. For trainees, this is a structured pathway to ACA qualification, combining hands\-on client work with mentorship from experienced advisors. You will be exposed to substantive technical matters from day one, rather than being confined to routine tasks, and will build a strong foundation across all areas of the practice. For qualified applicants, this is an opportunity to work on complex, high\-value engagements across tax advisory, forensic accounting, corporate restructuring, and litigation support, in a firm where technical quality and client relationships are taken seriously. **Responsibilities** * Prepare and review financial statements, management accounts, and statutory accounts for a range of clients including trading companies, holding structures, and investment vehicles. * Assist in the preparation of corporate and personal tax returns across CT, IT, CGT, CAT, VAT, and stamp duty, with an emphasis on accuracy, legislative compliance, and deadline management. * Support the preparation of detailed tax advisory letters covering areas such as share\-for\-share exchanges, corporate reconstructions, loss planning, entrepreneur relief, retirement relief, dwelling house relief, and related party transactions. * Assist in forensic accounting assignments, including preparation of statements of affairs, reconstruction of financial records, and analysis of financial data for use in litigation and court proceedings. * Contribute to company reconstruction and restructuring engagements, including intragroup transfers, share reorganisations, and holding company insertions, with reference to relevant TCA 1997 provisions and Revenue guidance. * Support Revenue audit and TAC appeal work, including preparation of submissions, analysis of disputed positions, and engagement with correspondence from Revenue. * Assist in the preparation of Capital Acquisitions Tax computations, CAT returns, and advisory notes for estate planning, will structures, and gift transactions. * Conduct VAT analysis across property transactions, Capital Goods Scheme matters, and cross\-border service arrangements, including EU VAT and place of supply issues. * Prepare board minutes, shareholder resolutions, and other company secretarial documentation in connection with corporate transactions. * Engage directly with clients, solicitors, barristers, and financial institutions in a professional and precise manner, representing the firm's standards in all written and verbal communication. * Maintain well\-organised client files and working paper packs suitable for internal review, Revenue inspection, or use as court evidence where required. **Qualifications and Skills** *Trainees* * Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Business, or a related discipline, or equivalent. * Currently pursuing or intending to pursue ACA qualification, with full study support provided by the firm. * A strong academic record and a genuine interest in tax and advisory work beyond routine compliance. * No prior practice experience required, though relevant placement or internship experience is an advantage. *Qualified Applicants* * ACA or ACCA qualified; CTA is a distinct advantage given the advisory nature of the role. * Good working knowledge of Irish tax legislation, TCA 1997, and Revenue practice. * Experience across a mix of compliance and advisory work, with an ability to handle complex, multi\-party client structures. *All Applicants* * Strong analytical ability and comfort working with detailed financial data, legislative text, and complex structures. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, with particular emphasis on Word and Excel. * Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, precise, and well\-structured letters and reports. * High attention to detail and the ability to manage competing deadlines across multiple client files. * A professional and discreet approach to client confidentiality, particularly in the context of litigation support and forensic assignments.

title: Underwriting Assistant
description: **Who We Are** American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets, manage risks and provide for retirement security. **Get to know the business** General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for commercial and personal insurance customers. It includes one of the world’s most far\-reaching property casualty networks. General Insurance offers a broad range of products to customers through a diversified, multichannel distribution network. **About The Role** **What you need to know:** This position will be in the Customer Service/Underwriting Support team within the Operations \& Service function. The role will focus on supporting and servicing all lines of Business across Consumer and Commercial. * Providing prompt \& accurate support to new and existing customers by phone, email and post. Attract potential customers by answering product and service questions; suggesting information about other products and services where opportunity arises. * Investigate and resolve routine customer enquiries at first point of contact, ensuring the delivery of an efficient and effective service to Customers and Brokers. * Analyze, screen and prepare Renewals for issuance * Responsible for actively ensuring the retention of the customer base which includes promoting policy benefits to existing customers. * Compile data and conduct routine tasks to support Underwriters. * Collate, filter and prepare data in preparation for Offshore processing and respond to queries arising from same. * Liaising with Claims and Finance Departments to resolve queries in a quick and efficient manner. * Produce reports on an ad hoc basis * Contribute to the development and maintenance of standards, policies and procedures regarding customer service. Maintain Company standard during all interactions * Quality review testing on both offshore and local Processes to ensure CPC compliance. * Participate in developing and recommending changes to existing methods and systems to increase the accuracy, efficiency and responsiveness of the customer service department as a whole. * Provide back\-up support to other team members. **What We’re Looking For** * Minimum one year’s experience working in the insurance industry * Hold a recognized MCC qualification (or be grandfathered) or be working towards a recognised qualification * Strong working knowledge of MS Office Applications * Strong written and oral communicational skills supported by a friendly and outgoing disposition * Methodical, accurate and strong attention to detail. * Organized, with good planning/time management skills to achieve tight deadlines/targets **A Look At Our Benefits** At AIG, we have a 100\-year legacy of working to make the world a better place. And that begins with our employees. We’re proud to offer a range of employee benefits and resources that help you protect what matters most – your health care, financial protection, and wellbeing. We provide a variety of leaves for personal, health, and family needs. For example, the “Giving Back” program allows you to take up to 16 hours a year to volunteer in your community. We also believe in fostering our employees’ development and offer a range of learning opportunities for employees to hone their professional skills to position themselves for the next steps of their careers. AIG also has a tuition reimbursement program for eligible employees to enhance their education, skills, and knowledge in areas that relate to their current position or future positions to which they may transfer or progress. **We are an Equal Opportunity Employer** It has been and will continue to be the policy of American International Group, Inc., its subsidiaries and affiliates to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We provide equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. At AIG, we believe that diversity and inclusion are critical to our future and our mission – creating a foundation for a creative workplace that leads to innovation, growth, and profitability. Through a wide variety of programs and initiatives, we invest in each employee, seeking to ensure that our people are not only respected as individuals, but also truly valued for their unique perspectives. \#AIG\-LI At AIG, we value in\-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. **Enjoy Benefits That Take Care Of What Matters** At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. **Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world** American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far\-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever\-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. **Welcome to a culture of inclusion** We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. *AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.* AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com. **Functional Area** OP \- Operations AIG Europe S.A. (Ireland branch)

title: Warehouse Operative
description: **About Us** Home to the iconic Instantor® Brand, Sanbra Fyffe is a market leader in the manufacturing, wholesale and distribution of plumbing supplies to the builder’s merchants trade for 90 years. At Instantor, our values aren’t just words on a page — they shape how we work, support each other, and deliver for our customers. We’re looking for someone who shares these values and wants to thrive in a collaborative, customer\-focused environment. **Teamwork:** All departments within the business act as one team to make Sanbra Fyffe the best it can be. **Customer Focus**: We commit to meet and exceed our customer’s expectations, by placing the customer at the centre of all our decisions. **Quality \& Reliability:** We focus on doing things the right way, the first time; delivering on quality, on time and on cost. **Expertise:** We develop and manufacture solutions to provide our customers with maximum value. **Role Purpose** We are now looking to recruit an additional Warehouse Operative where the role will be based out of our Santry depot where you will report to the Warehouse Manager and Operations Manager. The role will involve a full technical induction and training programme to enable the successful candidate to gain experience with the company’s product offering, allowing them to develop and best fulfil their potential. **Key Responsibilities** * Will ensure all sales orders are accurately picked in a timely manner and despatched according to agreed delivery schedules * Will keep stock in a tidy, orderly manner * Will ensure that no stock leaves stores without the proper paperwork and signatures required * Will handle occasional customer queries at the collections counter * Will alert managers to incomplete orders and advise to re\-order stock if not already on order on the system * Will carry out daily ABC stock taking exercises as required * Will use a forklift when required in accordance with standard forklift operating procedures * Will have basic computer skills i.e. ability to use email and master ‘Intact’ \- sales and inventory system * Will be open to variation in job role as required i.e. flexibility essential in line with needs of the company * Must be a team player, a good people person, have good customer service skills **Key Requirements** * Minimum two years warehouse / picking experience * Forklift / Bendy Experience a distinct advantage * Manual Handling Cert * A good level of physical fitness * Counterbalance license and experience preferred if possible. **Rota** Monday to Thursday: 7:00am – 3:45pm Fridays: 7:00am – 2:45pm for two weeks concurrently. Monday to Thursday: 10:00am – 6:45pm Fridays: 10:00am – 5:45pm on every third week **Benefits** · Competitive Salary · Employer Pension Contribution from start date · Employee Assistance Program (for you, your partner/spouse, and dependents 16\+) · Staff Discounts Learn more at Instantor.ie *Please Note – We do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies at this time.* *At Instantor we are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all.* Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €30,500\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Santry, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you currently hold an active Counterbalance license? * What are your salary expectations? * Please confirm you are available to work the shifts as outlined. Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse Operative
description: T **J O' Mahony Bluebell,** John F Kennedy Road, John F Kennedy Industrial Estate, Bluebell, Co Dublin, D12 KR94, part of HPC Sales Ltd., Irelands' leading Builders Providers and Home Improvement/DIY Store, is looking for a Full\-Time, Permanent, Warehouse Operative to join our Team **Job Purpose:** Warehouse Operative **.** **Duties and Responsibilities:** Pick and complete sales orders, wrap deliveries, complete warehouse paperwork for each dispatch. Unload, record and put away of stocks and deliveries Ensure all Health and Safety standards are always adhered to in line with Company policy. Load and unload lorries in a safe and efficient manner Move and stack materials Using correct manual handling techniques. Perform general warehouse duties in order to maintain a smooth operation Checking that finished product packs are closed/sealed properly and that labels are in the correct position Delivering product to customers by van may also be a requirement of the position **Requirements:** Good communication and interpersonal skills Computer Skills Friendly, helpful attitude Have full reach and counterbalance forklift licences **Benefits:** Career progression Bonus Scheme Bike to Work Scheme Free On\-Site Parking **Hours:** 40 hours per week (Monday \- Saturday) *Home Project Centre Sales Ltd. is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Warehouse Technician
description: **Warehouse Technician** **Location:** Dunboyne, Co. Meath **Contract:** 11\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract **Pay Rate:** €18\.29 per hour \+ 20% Shift Premium (applicable to evening shifts) **Work Arrangement:** Fully On\-Site **About the Role** We are currently recruiting for a **Warehouse Technician** to join a leading manufacturing facility in **Dunboyne** . This role is ideal for candidates with warehouse, production, manufacturing, or logistics experience who enjoy working in a fast\-paced and regulated environment. You will support day\-to\-day warehouse operations, ensuring materials are received, stored, moved, and dispatched safely and efficiently while maintaining high standards of quality and compliance. **Key Responsibilities** * **Receive** , inspect, and process incoming materials and deliveries. * Perform **material handling** , storage, and movement activities throughout the facility. * Complete **picking and kitting** activities to support manufacturing operations. * Load and unload **vehicles and deliveries** safely and efficiently. * Maintain accurate **inventory records** and support title: Warehouse Technician-Shift
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Summary:** The Warehouse Technician supports the Warehouse Team with the day\-to\-day operations in the Warehouse regarding materials sampling, materials movements into Site, within Site operational areas \& shipments from Site to both other Sites and external customers. This role will operate with an emphasis on safety and quality and will be committed to continuous improvement, teamwork, personal development and delivery of best\-in\-class service in support of Drug Substance manufacturing operations at the facility. The Warehouse Technician will work closely with both the larger Supply Chain team, as well as other Site departments and key external stakeholders. **Requirements** **Key Accountabilities:** * Execute Goods Receipting activities. * Execute Sampling activities using the American National Standards Institute (ANSI) method. * Execute Picking and Kitting activities for Manufacturing material requests. * Execute Cyle Counting activities. * Execute Outbound Shipping and Logistics activities, including dangerous goods. Support the pack out of Drug Substance. * Document material offload checks and sampling activities on eLogs. * Key user of the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, COMET (SAP). * Key user of the Laboratory Management system, GLIMS. * Support an environment of continuous improvement by participating in and driving innovative activities and ideas in the Material Availability hub to help improve the value stream. * Support Quality Event Investigations by providing Subject Matter Expert (SME) knowledge. * Act as a Qualified Trainer for Warehouse activities. * Always Maintains/ Supports a high standard of Safety /GMP compliance. * Actively seeks out performance improvement opportunities, through cross functional training \& experience. * Own Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) for an opertational area in the Warehouse and, where required, modify training packages. * Specialise in one operational area in the Warehouse: Cell Bank Management; Cold Chain Logistics; * Material Management; ERP System Superuser. * Actively participate in Audits and GEMBAs as an SME and, support Shopfloor problem\-solving. * Actively participate in the Material Availability Tier 0 Board and the Material Availability Tier 1 Board. * Any other duties assigned **Educational and Experience Requirements:** * 1\-2 years experience in a Good Manufacturing Production (GMP)\-regulated environment. * Forklift license is desirable. * Education Level 6 – minimum. * Ability to work independently and, within a cross\-functional team. * Experience using ERP systems at a manufacturing site. * Experience using Microsoft Office programs. * Ability to promote a culture where Diversity and Inclusion is supported. **Other Job Requirements:** * This role is a site\-based role. * This role requires shift work. The needs of business might need a change in shift pattern – both afternoon\-shift \& weekend support. * The shift is 16/5\. 7:00 am to 15:00 pm next week 15:00pm to 23:00 pm

title: Work Ready Graduate Programme: UX / UI / Learning Sciences Graduates
description: **Graduates in User Experience, Learner Experience, Learning Sciences and Instructional Design \- come work with us!** **Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme** * Edtech Industry work placement * Training \& One\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave * Building networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Duration: Full\-time, 1 year (August 2026 – August 2027\) \*\*\*\* Please note: **Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link** below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 **About Learnovate** Learnovate is an industry lead research and innovation centre focused on the Future of Work and Learning supported by Enterprise Ireland, the IDA and hosted by Trinity College Dublin. Our team has rich expertise in learning science, technology, user\-centric product design and customer\-centric innovation for over twelve years. We are excited to announce that applications are open for Year 3 of our Work Ready Graduate Programme, which aims to provide opportunities in the learning technology industry. What is the Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme? Graduates participate in a year\-long work placement hosted by a partner company and supported by the Learnovate team, employed by Trinity College Dublin. During the year the graduate will work in the company on a project focused on or involving a learning technology solution supporting the learner in either a corporate learning or a formal education environment. Throughout the 12 month placement the graduate is hosted by one of Learnovate’s partner companies and supported by a mentor in Learnovate, along with the following opportunities: * Formal skills training, networking and engagement opportunities * Business skills training for example accredited Project Management \& Scrum * One\-to\-one mentoring with Learnovate senior researcher * Access to the latest knowledge and technologies in regards to the future of work and learning * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts * Conference and events attendance **Benefits:** * Industry work placement * Structured training and one\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave **Eligibility Criteria:** * Masters Degree is desirable \- a Level 8 degree is acceptable in disciplines relevant to: * Learning sciences * Education * Learner experience/user experience design * Interest in/focus on: * Education * User and Learner Experience * Instructional Design * Eligible to work in Ireland (any visa required to work must be in place at the time of application) **The Programme includes:** * Formal training will include skills training, networking and engagement opportunities. * Informal training facilitated by the experience of participation in the Learnovate multi\-disciplinary team activities and exposure to the innovation methodologies in use. * Access to the latest knowledge and techniques within the current technical landscape in regards to the future of work and learning. * Thorough grounding and training in modern business and software design best practices * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts. If you fulfil the eligibility criteria and would like to be considered for participation in this programme, please fill out the following form for more information. Depending on the placement company, there may be opportunities for hybrid and remote working. \*\*\*\* Please note: Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 This is a 12 month placement, commencing in August, 2026 For any queries email gradapplications@learnovatecentre.org
